gc.computeGDOPAngular(point, coveringLinks)\n106\t\tresult.GDOP = gdop\n107\t\tresult.Quality = gdopToQuality(gdop)\n108\t\n109\t\treturn result\n110\t}\n111\t\n112\t// computeGDOPAngular computes GDOP based on angular diversity of link directions\n113\t// This is the 2D GDOP formula from the plan\n114\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) computeGDOPAngular(point Point, links []Link) float64 {\n115\t\t// Step 1: Collect link angles\n116\t\t// For each link, compute the angle of the line from TX to RX as seen from point\n117\t\ttype linkAngle struct {\n118\t\t\ttheta float64 // angle in radians\n119\t\t\tlink  Link\n120\t\t}\n121\t\n122\t\tangles := make([]linkAngle, 0, len(links))\n123\t\tfor _, link := range links {\n124\t\t\t// Project to floor plane (ignore Z for 2D analysis)\n125\t\t\tdx := link.RX.Position.X - link.TX.Position.X\n126\t\t\tdy := link.RX.Position.Y - link.TX.Position.Y\n127\t\t\ttheta := math.Atan2(dy, dx)\n128\t\t\tangles = append(angles, linkAngle{theta: theta, link: link})\n129\t\t}\n130\t\n131\t\tif len(angles) < 2 {\n132\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n133\t\t}\n134\t\n135\t\t// Step 2: Build Fisher information matrix\n136\t\t// F = Σ [ [cos²(θ),       cos(θ)·sin(θ)],\n137\t\t//        [cos(θ)·sin(θ), sin²(θ)       ] ]\n138\t\tvar f00, f01, f11 float64\n139\t\n140\t\tfor _, la := range angles {\n141\t\t\tc := math.Cos(la.theta)\n142\t\t\ts := math.Sin(la.theta)\n143\t\n144\t\t\tf00 += c * c\n145\t\t\tf01 += c * s\n146\t\t\tf11 += s * s\n147\t\t}\n148\t\n149\t\t// Step 3: Compute determinant\n150\t\tdet := f00*f11 - f01*f01\n151\t\n152\t\t// Check for degenerate geometry (collinear links)\n153\t\tif det <= 1e-6 {\n154\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n155\t\t}\n156\t\n157\t\t// Step 4: Compute trace of F^-1\n158\t\t// For 2x2 matrix: trace(F^-1) = (f00 + f11) / det\n159\t\ttraceFInv := (f00 + f11) / det\n160\t\n161\t\t// Step 5: GDOP = sqrt(trace(F^-1))\n162\t\tgdop := math.Sqrt(traceFInv)\n163\t\n164\t\treturn gdop\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// gdopToQuality converts GDOP value to quality string\n168\tfunc gdopToQuality(gdop float64) string {\n169\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n170\t\t\treturn \"none\"\n171\t\t}\n172\t\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n173\t\t\treturn \"excellent\"\n174\t\t}\n175\t\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n176\t\t\treturn \"good\"\n177\t\t}\n178\t\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n179\t\t\treturn \"fair\"\n180\t\t}\n181\t\treturn \"poor\"\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// CoverageScore computes the percentage of cells with \"good\" or better coverage\n185\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) CoverageScore(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n186\t\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n187\t\t\treturn 0\n188\t\t}\n189\t\n190\t\tgoodCells := 0\n191\t\ttotalCells := 0\n192\t\n193\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n194\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n195\t\t\t\ttotalCells++\n196\t\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"excellent\" || cell.Quality == \"good\" {\n197\t\t\t\t\tgoodCells++\n198\t\t\t\t}\n199\t\t\t}\n200\t\t}\n201\t\n202\t\treturn 100.0 * float64(goodCells) / float64(totalCells)\n203\t}\n204\t\n205\t// AverageGDOP computes the average GDOP over all cells (excluding infinity)\n206\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) AverageGDOP(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n207\t\tsum := 0.0\n208\t\tcount := 0\n209\t\n210\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n211\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n212\t\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(cell.GDOP, 0) {\n213\t\t\t\t\tsum += cell.GDOP\n214\t\t\t\t\tcount++\n215\t\t\t\t}\n216\t\t\t}\n217\t\t}\n218\t\n219\t\tif count == 0 {\n220\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n221\t\t}\n222\t\treturn sum / float64(count)\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\t// QualityCounts returns the count of cells by quality level\n226\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) QualityCounts(results [][]GDOPResult) map[string]int {\n227\t\tcounts := map[string]int{\n228\t\t\t\"excellent\": 0,\n229\t\t\t\"good\":      0,\n230\t\t\t\"fair\":      0,\n231\t\t\t\"poor\":      0,\n232\t\t\t\"none\":      0,\n233\t\t}\n234\t\n235\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n236\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n237\t\t\t\tcounts[cell.Quality]++\n238\t\t\t}\n239\t\t}\n240\t\n241\t\treturn counts\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t// FindDeadZones returns positions where coverage is \"none\" or \"poor\"\n245\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) FindDeadZones(results [][]GDOPResult) []Point {\n246\t\tdeadZones := make([]Point, 0)\n247\t\n248\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n249\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n250\t\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"none\" || cell.Quality == \"poor\" {\n251\t\t\t\t\tdeadZones = append(deadZones, Point{X: cell.X, Y: cell.Y, Z: cell.Z})\n252\t\t\t\t}\n253\t\t\t}\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn deadZones\n257\t}\n258\t\n259\t// RecommendNodePosition suggests optimal positions for additional nodes\n260\t// based on current dead zones\n261\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) RecommendNodePosition(results [][]GDOPResult, space *Space) Point {\n262\t\t// Find the centroid of the largest dead zone\n263\t\tdeadZones := gc.FindDeadZones(results)\n264\t\tif len(deadZones) == 0 {\n265\t\t\t// No dead zones, suggest center of space\n266\t\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n267\t\t\treturn Point{\n268\t\t\t\tX: (minX + maxX) / 2,\n269\t\t\t\tY: (minY + maxY) / 2,\n270\t\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement\n271\t\t\t}\n272\t\t}\n273\t\n274\t\t// Cluster dead zones and find the largest cluster\n275\t\t// Simplified: just return the centroid of all dead zones\n276\t\tvar sumX, sumY float64\n277\t\tfor _, dz := range deadZones {\n278\t\t\tsumX += dz.X\n279\t\t\tsumY += dz.Y\n280\t\t}\n281\t\n282\t\tcentroid := Point{\n283\t\t\tX: sumX / float64(len(deadZones)),\n284\t\t\tY: sumY / float64(len(deadZones)),\n285\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement for better coverage\n286\t\t}\n287\t\n288\t\treturn centroid\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t// MinimumNodeCount estimates the minimum number of nodes needed for good coverage\n292\t// Based on space dimensions and desired quality threshold\n293\tfunc MinimumNodeCount(space *Space, targetGDOP float64) int {\n294\t\twidth, depth, _ := space.Dimensions()\n295\t\tarea := width * depth\n296\t\n297\t\t// Heuristic: nodes needed based on area and desired GDOP\n298\t\t// For GDOP < 4 (good coverage): approximately 1 node per 15-20 m²\n299\t\tif targetGDOP < 2 {\n300\t\t\t// Excellent coverage requires more nodes\n301\t\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 15.0))\n302\t\t}\n303\t\tif targetGDOP < 4 {\n304\t\t\t// Good coverage\n305\t\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 20.0))\n306\t\t}\n307\t\t// Fair coverage\n308\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 30.0))\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// ExpectedAccuracy estimates the expected localization accuracy at a point\n312\t// based on its GDOP value\n313\tfunc ExpectedAccuracy(gdop float64) float64 {\n314\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n315\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\t// Based on research: typical CSI accuracy with 4+ nodes is ±0.5-1.0m\n319\t\t// GDOP < 2: ±0.5m, GDOP 2-4: ±1.0m, GDOP > 4: degrades further\n320\t\tbaseAccuracy := 0.5 // meters for GDOP = 1\n321\t\n322\t\treturn baseAccuracy * gdop\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\t// GDOPColor represents a color for GDOP visualization\n326\ttype GDOPColor struct {\n327\t\tR, G, B uint8 // RGB values 0-255\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t// GDOPColorMap returns the color for a given GDOP value for visualization\n331\t// Uses: green (excellent), yellow (good), orange (fair), red (poor), gray (none)\n332\tfunc GDOPColorMap(gdop float64) GDOPColor {\n333\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n334\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 80, G: 80, B: 80} // Gray for no coverage\n335\t\t}\n336\t\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n337\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 34, G: 197, B: 94} // Green (#22c65e) for excellent\n338\t\t}\n339\t\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n340\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 193, B: 7} // Yellow (#ffc107) for good\n341\t\t}\n342\t\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n343\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 146, B: 0} // Orange (#ff9200) for fair\n344\t\t}\n345\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 220, G: 53, B: 69} // Red (#dc3545) for poor\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// GDOPHeatmapData represents flattened GDOP data for frontend rendering\n349\ttype GDOPHeatmapData struct {\n350\t\tWidth      int         `json:\"width\"`       // Grid width (columns)\n351\t\tDepth      int         `json:\"depth\"`       // Grid depth (rows)\n352\t\tCellSize   float64     `json:\"cell_size\"`   // Cell size in meters\n353\t\tOriginX    float64     `json:\"origin_x\"`    // Grid origin X\n354\t\tOriginY    float64     `json:\"origin_y\"`    // Grid origin Y\n355\t\tGDOPValues []float64   `json:\"gdop_values\"` // Flattened GDOP values (9999 = infinity)\n356\t\tQualities  []string    `json:\"qualities\"`   // Flattened quality strings\n357\t\tColors     [][]uint8   `json:\"colors\"`      // Flattened RGB colors [width*depth*3]\n358\t\tAccuracyMap []float64   `json:\"accuracy_map\"` // Expected accuracy in meters per cell\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t// ToHeatmapData converts GDOP results to a heatmap-friendly format\n362\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ToHeatmapData(results [][]GDOPResult) *GDOPHeatmapData {\n363\t\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n364\t\t\treturn &GDOPHeatmapData{}\n365\t\t}\n366\t\n367\t\tdepth := len(results)    // rows (Y)\n368\t\twidth := len(results[0]) // cols (X)\n369\t\ttotalCells := width * depth\n370\t\n371\t\tdata := &GDOPHeatmapData{\n372\t\t\tWidth:       width,\n373\t\t\tDepth:       depth,\n374\t\t\tCellSize:    gc.config.CellSize,\n375\t\t\tOriginX:     gc.config.MinX,\n376\t\t\tOriginY:     gc.config.MinY,\n377\t\t\tGDOPValues:  make([]float64, totalCells),\n378\t\t\tQualities:   make([]string, totalCells),\n379\t\t\tColors:      make([][]uint8, totalCells),\n380\t\t\tAccuracyMap: make([]float64, totalCells),\n381\t\t}\n382\t\n383\t\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n384\t\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n385\t\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n386\t\t\t\tresult := results[y][x]\n387\t\n388\t\t\t\t// GDOP value (9999 for infinity)\n389\t\t\t\tif math.IsInf(result.GDOP, 0) {\n390\t\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = 9999.0\n391\t\t\t\t} else {\n392\t\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = result.GDOP\n393\t\t\t\t}\n394\t\n395\t\t\t\t// Quality string\n396\t\t\t\tdata.Qualities[idx] = result.Quality\n397\t\n398\t\t\t\t// RGB color\n399\t\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(result.GDOP)\n400\t\t\t\tdata.Colors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n401\t\n402\t\t\t\t// Expected accuracy (use 9999.0 for infinity)\n403\t\t\t\taccuracy := ExpectedAccuracy(result.GDOP)\n404\t\t\t\tif math.IsInf(accuracy, 0) {\n405\t\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = 9999.0\n406\t\t\t\t} else {\n407\t\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = accuracy\n408\t\t\t\t}\n409\t\t\t}\n410\t\t}\n411\t\n412\t\treturn data\n413\t}\n414\t\n415\t// ComputeAccuracyMap computes expected accuracy for each cell\n416\t// Returns a 2D array of accuracy values in meters (infinity = no coverage)\n417\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAccuracyMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]float64 {\n418\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n419\t\t\treturn nil\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\taccuracyMap := make([][]float64, len(results))\n423\t\tfor i := range results {\n424\t\t\taccuracyMap[i] = make([]float64, len(results[i]))\n425\t\t\tfor j := range results[i] {\n426\t\t\t\taccuracyMap[i][j] = ExpectedAccuracy(results[i][j].GDOP)\n427\t\t\t}\n428\t\t}\n429\t\n430\t\treturn accuracyMap\n431\t}\n432\t\n433\t// ComputeColorMap computes RGB colors for each cell for visualization\n434\t// Returns a flattened array of RGB values [width*depth*3]\n435\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeColorMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]uint8 {\n436\t\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n437\t\t\treturn nil\n438\t\t}\n439\t\n440\t\tdepth := len(results)\n441\t\twidth := len(results[0])\n442\t\ttotalCells := width * depth\n443\t\n444\t\tcolors := make([][]uint8, totalCells)\n445\t\n446\t\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n447\t\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n448\t\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n449\t\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(results[y][x].GDOP)\n450\t\t\t\tcolors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n451\t\t\t}\n452\t\t}\n453\t\n454\t\treturn colors\n455\t}\n456\t\n457\t// GetWorstCoverageCells returns the N cells with the worst GDOP values\n458\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetWorstCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n459\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n460\t\t\treturn nil\n461\t\t}\n462\t\n463\t\t// Flatten all cells\n464\t\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n465\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n466\t\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n467\t\t}\n468\t\n469\t\t// Sort by GDOP (descending, so worst first)\n470\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n471\t\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n472\t\t\t\t// Handle infinity: infinity is worse than any finite value\n473\t\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n474\t\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n475\t\n476\t\t\t\tvar swap bool\n477\t\t\t\tif iInf && !jInf {\n478\t\t\t\t\tswap = false // i stays (infinity at top)\n479\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && jInf {\n480\t\t\t\t\tswap = true // j is infinity, should be before i\n481\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n482\t\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP > cells[i].GDOP\n483\t\t\t\t}\n484\t\n485\t\t\t\tif swap {\n486\t\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n487\t\t\t\t}\n488\t\t\t}\n489\t\t}\n490\t\n491\t\t// Return top N worst cells\n492\t\tif n > len(cells) {\n493\t\t\tn = len(cells)\n494\t\t}\n495\t\treturn cells[:n]\n496\t}\n497\t\n498\t// GetBestCoverageCells returns the N cells with the best GDOP values\n499\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetBestCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n500\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n501\t\t\treturn nil\n502\t\t}\n503\t\n504\t\t// Flatten all cells\n505\t\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n506\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n507\t\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n508\t\t}\n509\t\n510\t\t// Sort by GDOP (ascending, so best first)\n511\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n512\t\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n513\t\t\t\t// Handle infinity: finite values are better than infinity\n514\t\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n515\t\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n516\t\n517\t\t\t\tvar swap bool\n518\t\t\t\tif !iInf && jInf {\n519\t\t\t\t\tswap = false // i is finite, j is infinity, i is better\n520\t\t\t\t} else if iInf && !jInf {\n521\t\t\t\t\tswap = true // i is infinity, j is finite, j should be before i\n522\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n523\t\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP < cells[i].GDOP\n524\t\t\t\t}\n525\t\n526\t\t\t\tif swap {\n527\t\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n528\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t}\n530\t\t}\n531\t\n532\t\t// Return top N best cells\n533\t\tif n > len(cells) {\n534\t\t\tn = len(cells)\n535\t\t}\n536\t\treturn cells[:n]\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\t// OptimizeNodePositions uses a greedy algorithm to find better node positions\n540\t// for a given number of nodes within the space\n541\tfunc OptimizeNodePositions(space *Space, numNodes int, iterations int) *NodeSet {\n542\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n543\t\n544\t\t// Start with corner positions\n545\t\tbestSet := NewNodeSet()\n546\t\tcorners := CornerPositions(space)\n547\t\n548\t\tfor i := 0; i < numNodes; i++ {\n549\t\t\tif i < len(corners) {\n550\t\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n551\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n552\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n553\t\t\t\t\tcorners[i],\n554\t\t\t\t)\n555\t\t\t} else {\n556\t\t\t\t// Add random position (Go 1.20+ automatically seeds global generator)\n557\t\t\t\tpos := Point{\n558\t\t\t\t\tX: minX + mrand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n559\t\t\t\t\tY: minY + mrand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n560\t\t\t\t\tZ: mrand.Float64() * maxZ,\n561\t\t\t\t}\n562\t\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n563\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n564\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n565\t\t\t\t\tpos,\n566\t\t\t\t)\n567\t\t\t}\n568\t\t}\n569\t\n570\t\t// Generate initial links and compute coverage\n571\t\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(bestSet)\n572\t\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n573\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n574\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n575\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n576\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n577\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n578\t\t})\n579\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n580\t\tbestScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n581\t\n582\t\t// Iterative improvement\n583\t\tfor iter := 0; iter < iterations; iter++ {\n584\t\t\t// Try moving each node slightly\n585\t\t\tfor i, node := range bestSet.All() {\n586\t\t\t\t// Generate candidate position\n587\t\t\t\tcandidatePos := Point{\n588\t\t\t\t\tX: node.Position.X + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0, // ±0.5m\n589\t\t\t\t\tY: node.Position.Y + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0,\n590\t\t\t\t\tZ: node.Position.Z + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*0.5, // Less Z variation\n591\t\t\t\t}\n592\t\n593\t\t\t\t// Keep within bounds\n594\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.X = math.Max(minX, math.Min(maxX, candidatePos.X))\n595\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.Y = math.Max(minY, math.Min(maxY, candidatePos.Y))\n596\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.Z = math.Max(0, math.Min(maxZ, candidatePos.Z))\n597\t\n598\t\t\t\t// Create test set with this node moved\n599\t\t\t\ttestSet := NewNodeSet()\n600\t\t\t\tfor j, n := range bestSet.All() {\n601\t\t\t\t\tif j == i {\n602\t\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.AddVirtualNode(n.ID, n.Name, candidatePos)\n603\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n604\t\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.Add(n)\n605\t\t\t\t\t}\n606\t\t\t\t}\n607\t\n608\t\t\t\t// Evaluate\n609\t\t\t\ttestLinks := GenerateAllLinks(testSet)\n610\t\t\t\ttestGDOP := NewGDOPComputer(testLinks, GridConfig{\n611\t\t\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n612\t\t\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n613\t\t\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n614\t\t\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n615\t\t\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n616\t\t\t\t})\n617\t\t\t\ttestResults := testGDOP.ComputeAll()\n618\t\t\t\ttestScore := testGDOP.CoverageScore(testResults)\n619\t\n620\t\t\t\t// Keep if better\n621\t\t\t\tif testScore > bestScore {\n622\t\t\t\t\tbestScore = testScore\n623\t\t\t\t\tbestSet.All()[i].Position = candidatePos\n624\t\t\t\t}\n625\t\t\t}\n626\t\t}\n627\t\n628\t\treturn bestSet\n629\t}\n630\t\n631\t// GenerateShoppingList creates a shopping list from simulation results\n632\tfunc GenerateShoppingList(space *Space, currentNodes *NodeSet) *ShoppingList {\n633\t\tnodes := currentNodes\n634\t\tif nodes == nil || nodes.Count() == 0 {\n635\t\t\tnodes = SuggestedNodes(space, 4)\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n639\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n640\t\n641\t\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n642\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n643\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n644\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n645\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n646\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n647\t\t})\n648\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n649\t\n650\t\tcoverage := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n651\t\tavgGDOP := gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results)\n652\t\n653\t\treturn &ShoppingList{\n654\t\t\tMinimumNodes:     MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n655\t\t\tRecommendedNodes: nodes.Count(),\n656\t\t\tExpectedAccuracy: ExpectedAccuracy(avgGDOP),\n657\t\t\tCoveragePercent:  coverage,\n658\t\t\tOptimalPositions: extractNodePositions(nodes),\n659\t\t}\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\tfunc extractNodePositions(nodes *NodeSet) []Point {\n663\t\tpositions := make([]Point, 0, nodes.Count())\n664\t\tfor _, n := range nodes.All() {\n665\t\t\tpositions = append(positions, n.Position)\n666\t\t}\n667\t\treturn positions\n668\t}\n669\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b3b417b-ffca-4e51-853f-9cbdc68e2df1","uuid":"33e16072-466c-4aa4-af0b-47c8c85b8e8a","timestamp":"2026-05-06T03:27:37.960Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/simulator/gdop.go","content":"package simulator\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\tmrand \"math/rand\"\n)\n\n// GDOPResult contains GDOP computation results for a single cell\ntype GDOPResult struct {\n\tX, Y, Z      float64  // Cell center position\n\tGDOP         float64  // Computed GDOP value (Infinity = no coverage)\n\tQuality      string   // \"excellent\", \"good\", \"fair\", \"poor\", \"none\"\n\tContributingLinks []string // Link IDs that contributed to this cell\n}\n\n// GridConfig defines the GDOP computation grid\ntype GridConfig struct {\n\tCellSize    float64 // Grid cell size in meters\n\tMinX, MinY  float64 // Grid origin\n\tWidth       float64 // Grid width\n\tDepth       float64 // Grid depth\n}\n\n// GDOPComputer computes Geometric Dilution of Precision for coverage analysis\ntype GDOPComputer struct {\n\tlinks     []Link\n\tconfig    GridConfig\n\tmaxZone   int // Maximum Fresnel zone to consider (default 3)\n}\n\n// NewGDOPComputer creates a new GDOP computer\nfunc NewGDOPComputer(links []Link, config GridConfig) *GDOPComputer {\n\tif config.CellSize <= 0 {\n\t\tconfig.CellSize = 0.2 // Default 20cm\n\t}\n\treturn &GDOPComputer{\n\t\tlinks:   links,\n\t\tconfig:  config,\n\t\tmaxZone: 3, // Default: consider first 3 Fresnel zones\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetMaxZone sets the maximum Fresnel zone to consider\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) SetMaxZone(zone int) {\n\tif zone < 1 {\n\t\tzone = 1\n\t}\n\tgc.maxZone = zone\n}\n\n// ComputeAll computes GDOP for the entire grid\n// Returns a slice of GDOP results indexed by cell position\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAll() [][]GDOPResult {\n\tnx := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Width / gc.config.CellSize))\n\tny := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Depth / gc.config.CellSize))\n\n\tresults := make([][]GDOPResult, ny)\n\n\tfor iy := 0; iy < ny; iy++ {\n\t\tresults[iy] = make([]GDOPResult, nx)\n\t\tfor ix := 0; ix < nx; ix++ {\n\t\t\tx := gc.config.MinX + (float64(ix)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n\t\t\ty := gc.config.MinY + (float64(iy)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n\t\t\tz := 1.0 // Use 1m height for 2D GDOP analysis\n\n\t\t\tresult := gc.ComputeAt(x, y, z)\n\t\t\tresults[iy][ix] = result\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn results\n}\n\n// ComputeAt computes GDOP at a specific point\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAt(x, y, z float64) GDOPResult {\n\tpoint := Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: z}\n\n\t// Collect links that cover this point (within maxZone Fresnel zones)\n\tvar coveringLinks []Link\n\tvar linkIDs []string\n\n\tfor _, link := range gc.links {\n\t\tif IsInFresnelZones(link.TX.Position, link.RX.Position, point, gc.maxZone) {\n\t\t\tcoveringLinks = append(coveringLinks, link)\n\t\t\tlinkIDs = append(linkIDs, link.TX.ID+\":\"+link.RX.ID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult := GDOPResult{\n\t\tX:                 x,\n\t\tY:                 y,\n\t\tZ:                 z,\n\t\tContributingLinks: linkIDs,\n\t}\n\n\tif len(coveringLinks) < 2 {\n\t\t// Need at least 2 links for 2D localization\n\t\tresult.GDOP = math.Inf(1)\n\t\tresult.Quality = \"none\"\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute GDOP using angular diversity\n\tgdop := gc.computeGDOPAngular(point, coveringLinks)\n\tresult.GDOP = gdop\n\tresult.Quality = gdopToQuality(gdop)\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// computeGDOPAngular computes GDOP based on angular diversity of link directions\n// This is the 2D GDOP formula from the plan\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) computeGDOPAngular(point Point, links []Link) float64 {\n\t// Step 1: Collect link angles\n\t// For each link, compute the angle of the line from TX to RX as seen from point\n\ttype linkAngle struct {\n\t\ttheta float64 // angle in radians\n\t\tlink  Link\n\t}\n\n\tangles := make([]linkAngle, 0, len(links))\n\tfor _, link := range links {\n\t\t// Project to floor plane (ignore Z for 2D analysis)\n\t\tdx := link.RX.Position.X - link.TX.Position.X\n\t\tdy := link.RX.Position.Y - link.TX.Position.Y\n\t\ttheta := math.Atan2(dy, dx)\n\t\tangles = append(angles, linkAngle{theta: theta, link: link})\n\t}\n\n\tif len(angles) < 2 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 2: Build Fisher information matrix\n\t// F = Σ [ [cos²(θ),       cos(θ)·sin(θ)],\n\t//        [cos(θ)·sin(θ), sin²(θ)       ] ]\n\tvar f00, f01, f11 float64\n\n\tfor _, la := range angles {\n\t\tc := math.Cos(la.theta)\n\t\ts := math.Sin(la.theta)\n\n\t\tf00 += c * c\n\t\tf01 += c * s\n\t\tf11 += s * s\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 3: Compute determinant\n\tdet := f00*f11 - f01*f01\n\n\t// Check for degenerate geometry (collinear links)\n\tif det <= 1e-6 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 4: Compute trace of F^-1\n\t// For 2x2 matrix: trace(F^-1) = (f00 + f11) / det\n\ttraceFInv := (f00 + f11) / det\n\n\t// Step 5: GDOP = sqrt(trace(F^-1))\n\tgdop := math.Sqrt(traceFInv)\n\n\treturn gdop\n}\n\n// gdopToQuality converts GDOP value to quality string\nfunc gdopToQuality(gdop float64) string {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn \"none\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n\t\treturn \"excellent\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n\t\treturn \"good\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n\t\treturn \"fair\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"poor\"\n}\n\n// CoverageScore computes the percentage of cells with \"good\" or better coverage\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) CoverageScore(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\n\tgoodCells := 0\n\ttotalCells := 0\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\ttotalCells++\n\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"excellent\" || cell.Quality == \"good\" {\n\t\t\t\tgoodCells++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn 100.0 * float64(goodCells) / float64(totalCells)\n}\n\n// AverageGDOP computes the average GDOP over all cells (excluding infinity)\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) AverageGDOP(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n\tsum := 0.0\n\tcount := 0\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(cell.GDOP, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tsum += cell.GDOP\n\t\t\t\tcount++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif count == 0 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\treturn sum / float64(count)\n}\n\n// QualityCounts returns the count of cells by quality level\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) QualityCounts(results [][]GDOPResult) map[string]int {\n\tcounts := map[string]int{\n\t\t\"excellent\": 0,\n\t\t\"good\":      0,\n\t\t\"fair\":      0,\n\t\t\"poor\":      0,\n\t\t\"none\":      0,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tcounts[cell.Quality]++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn counts\n}\n\n// FindDeadZones returns positions where coverage is \"none\" or \"poor\"\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) FindDeadZones(results [][]GDOPResult) []Point {\n\tdeadZones := make([]Point, 0)\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"none\" || cell.Quality == \"poor\" {\n\t\t\t\tdeadZones = append(deadZones, Point{X: cell.X, Y: cell.Y, Z: cell.Z})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn deadZones\n}\n\n// RecommendNodePosition suggests optimal positions for additional nodes\n// based on current dead zones\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) RecommendNodePosition(results [][]GDOPResult, space *Space) Point {\n\t// Find the centroid of the largest dead zone\n\tdeadZones := gc.FindDeadZones(results)\n\tif len(deadZones) == 0 {\n\t\t// No dead zones, suggest center of space\n\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\t\treturn Point{\n\t\t\tX: (minX + maxX) / 2,\n\t\t\tY: (minY + maxY) / 2,\n\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Cluster dead zones and find the largest cluster\n\t// Simplified: just return the centroid of all dead zones\n\tvar sumX, sumY float64\n\tfor _, dz := range deadZones {\n\t\tsumX += dz.X\n\t\tsumY += dz.Y\n\t}\n\n\tcentroid := Point{\n\t\tX: sumX / float64(len(deadZones)),\n\t\tY: sumY / float64(len(deadZones)),\n\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement for better coverage\n\t}\n\n\treturn centroid\n}\n\n// MinimumNodeCount estimates the minimum number of nodes needed for good coverage\n// Based on space dimensions and desired quality threshold\nfunc MinimumNodeCount(space *Space, targetGDOP float64) int {\n\twidth, depth, _ := space.Dimensions()\n\tarea := width * depth\n\n\t// Heuristic: nodes needed based on area and desired GDOP\n\t// For GDOP < 4 (good coverage): approximately 1 node per 15-20 m²\n\tif targetGDOP < 2 {\n\t\t// Excellent coverage requires more nodes\n\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 15.0))\n\t}\n\tif targetGDOP < 4 {\n\t\t// Good coverage\n\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 20.0))\n\t}\n\t// Fair coverage\n\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 30.0))\n}\n\n// ExpectedAccuracy estimates the expected localization accuracy at a point\n// based on its GDOP value\nfunc ExpectedAccuracy(gdop float64) float64 {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Based on research: typical CSI accuracy with 4+ nodes is ±0.5-1.0m\n\t// GDOP < 2: ±0.5m, GDOP 2-4: ±1.0m, GDOP > 4: degrades further\n\tbaseAccuracy := 0.5 // meters for GDOP = 1\n\n\treturn baseAccuracy * gdop\n}\n\n// GDOPColor represents a color for GDOP visualization\ntype GDOPColor struct {\n\tR, G, B uint8 // RGB values 0-255\n}\n\n// GDOPColorMap returns the color for a given GDOP value for visualization\n// Uses: green (excellent), yellow (good), orange (fair), red (poor), gray (none)\nfunc GDOPColorMap(gdop float64) GDOPColor {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 80, G: 80, B: 80} // Gray for no coverage\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 34, G: 197, B: 94} // Green (#22c65e) for excellent\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 193, B: 7} // Yellow (#ffc107) for good\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 146, B: 0} // Orange (#ff9200) for fair\n\t}\n\treturn GDOPColor{R: 220, G: 53, B: 69} // Red (#dc3545) for poor\n}\n\n// GDOPHeatmapData represents flattened GDOP data for frontend rendering\ntype GDOPHeatmapData struct {\n\tWidth      int         `json:\"width\"`       // Grid width (columns)\n\tDepth      int         `json:\"depth\"`       // Grid depth (rows)\n\tCellSize   float64     `json:\"cell_size\"`   // Cell size in meters\n\tOriginX    float64     `json:\"origin_x\"`    // Grid origin X\n\tOriginY    float64     `json:\"origin_y\"`    // Grid origin Y\n\tGDOPValues []float64   `json:\"gdop_values\"` // Flattened GDOP values (9999 = infinity)\n\tQualities  []string    `json:\"qualities\"`   // Flattened quality strings\n\tColors     [][]uint8   `json:\"colors\"`      // Flattened RGB colors [width*depth*3]\n\tAccuracyMap []float64   `json:\"accuracy_map\"` // Expected accuracy in meters per cell\n}\n\n// ToHeatmapData converts GDOP results to a heatmap-friendly format\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ToHeatmapData(results [][]GDOPResult) *GDOPHeatmapData {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn &GDOPHeatmapData{}\n\t}\n\n\tdepth := len(results)    // rows (Y)\n\twidth := len(results[0]) // cols (X)\n\ttotalCells := width * depth\n\n\tdata := &GDOPHeatmapData{\n\t\tWidth:       width,\n\t\tDepth:       depth,\n\t\tCellSize:    gc.config.CellSize,\n\t\tOriginX:     gc.config.MinX,\n\t\tOriginY:     gc.config.MinY,\n\t\tGDOPValues:  make([]float64, totalCells),\n\t\tQualities:   make([]string, totalCells),\n\t\tColors:      make([][]uint8, totalCells),\n\t\tAccuracyMap: make([]float64, totalCells),\n\t}\n\n\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n\t\t\tresult := results[y][x]\n\n\t\t\t// GDOP value (9999 for infinity)\n\t\t\tif math.IsInf(result.GDOP, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = 9999.0\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = result.GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Quality string\n\t\t\tdata.Qualities[idx] = result.Quality\n\n\t\t\t// RGB color\n\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(result.GDOP)\n\t\t\tdata.Colors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n\n\t\t\t// Expected accuracy (use 9999.0 for infinity)\n\t\t\taccuracy := ExpectedAccuracy(result.GDOP)\n\t\t\tif math.IsInf(accuracy, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = 9999.0\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = accuracy\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn data\n}\n\n// ComputeAccuracyMap computes expected accuracy for each cell\n// Returns a 2D array of accuracy values in meters (infinity = no coverage)\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAccuracyMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]float64 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\taccuracyMap := make([][]float64, len(results))\n\tfor i := range results {\n\t\taccuracyMap[i] = make([]float64, len(results[i]))\n\t\tfor j := range results[i] {\n\t\t\taccuracyMap[i][j] = ExpectedAccuracy(results[i][j].GDOP)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn accuracyMap\n}\n\n// ComputeColorMap computes RGB colors for each cell for visualization\n// Returns a flattened array of RGB values [width*depth*3]\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeColorMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]uint8 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tdepth := len(results)\n\twidth := len(results[0])\n\ttotalCells := width * depth\n\n\tcolors := make([][]uint8, totalCells)\n\n\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(results[y][x].GDOP)\n\t\t\tcolors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn colors\n}\n\n// GetWorstCoverageCells returns the N cells with the worst GDOP values\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetWorstCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Flatten all cells\n\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort by GDOP (descending, so worst first)\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n\t\t\t// Handle infinity: infinity is worse than any finite value\n\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n\n\t\t\tvar swap bool\n\t\t\tif iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = false // i stays (infinity at top)\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = true // j is infinity, should be before i\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP > cells[i].GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif swap {\n\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return top N worst cells\n\tif n > len(cells) {\n\t\tn = len(cells)\n\t}\n\treturn cells[:n]\n}\n\n// GetBestCoverageCells returns the N cells with the best GDOP values\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetBestCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Flatten all cells\n\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort by GDOP (ascending, so best first)\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n\t\t\t// Handle infinity: finite values are better than infinity\n\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n\n\t\t\tvar swap bool\n\t\t\tif !iInf && jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = false // i is finite, j is infinity, i is better\n\t\t\t} else if iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = true // i is infinity, j is finite, j should be before i\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP < cells[i].GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif swap {\n\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return top N best cells\n\tif n > len(cells) {\n\t\tn = len(cells)\n\t}\n\treturn cells[:n]\n}\n\n// OptimizeNodePositions uses a greedy algorithm to find better node positions\n// for a given number of nodes within the space\nfunc OptimizeNodePositions(space *Space, numNodes int, iterations int) *NodeSet {\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n\n\t// Start with corner positions\n\tbestSet := NewNodeSet()\n\tcorners := CornerPositions(space)\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < numNodes; i++ {\n\t\tif i < len(corners) {\n\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tcorners[i],\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Add random position (Go 1.20+ automatically seeds global generator)\n\t\t\tpos := Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: minX + mrand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n\t\t\t\tY: minY + mrand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n\t\t\t\tZ: mrand.Float64() * maxZ,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tpos,\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate initial links and compute coverage\n\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(bestSet)\n\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t})\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\tbestScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n\n\t// Iterative improvement\n\tfor iter := 0; iter < iterations; iter++ {\n\t\t// Try moving each node slightly\n\t\tfor i, node := range bestSet.All() {\n\t\t\t// Generate candidate position\n\t\t\tcandidatePos := Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: node.Position.X + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0, // ±0.5m\n\t\t\t\tY: node.Position.Y + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0,\n\t\t\t\tZ: node.Position.Z + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*0.5, // Less Z variation\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Keep within bounds\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.X = math.Max(minX, math.Min(maxX, candidatePos.X))\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.Y = math.Max(minY, math.Min(maxY, candidatePos.Y))\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.Z = math.Max(0, math.Min(maxZ, candidatePos.Z))\n\n\t\t\t// Create test set with this node moved\n\t\t\ttestSet := NewNodeSet()\n\t\t\tfor j, n := range bestSet.All() {\n\t\t\t\tif j == i {\n\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.AddVirtualNode(n.ID, n.Name, candidatePos)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.Add(n)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Evaluate\n\t\t\ttestLinks := GenerateAllLinks(testSet)\n\t\t\ttestGDOP := NewGDOPComputer(testLinks, GridConfig{\n\t\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\ttestResults := testGDOP.ComputeAll()\n\t\t\ttestScore := testGDOP.CoverageScore(testResults)\n\n\t\t\t// Keep if better\n\t\t\tif testScore > bestScore {\n\t\t\t\tbestScore = testScore\n\t\t\t\tbestSet.All()[i].Position = candidatePos\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn bestSet\n}\n\n// GenerateShoppingList creates a shopping list from simulation results\nfunc GenerateShoppingList(space *Space, currentNodes *NodeSet) *ShoppingList {\n\tnodes := currentNodes\n\tif nodes == nil || nodes.Count() == 0 {\n\t\tnodes = SuggestedNodes(space, 4)\n\t}\n\n\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\n\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - RolePassive\n301\t\t}\n302\t\n303\t\t// Regular TX to RX/TX_RX\n304\t\tif tx.Role == RoleTX {\n305\t\t\treturn rx.Role == RoleRX || rx.Role == RoleTXRX\n306\t\t}\n307\t\n308\t\t// TX_RX can both TX a heuristic based on space dimensions.\n329\t// Note: The actual implementation is in gdop.go to avoid duplication.\n330\tfunc MinimumNodeCountFromNode(s *Space, targetGDOP float64) int {\n331\t\twidth, depth, _ := s.Dimensions()\n332\t\tarea := width * depth\n333\t\n334\t\t// Heuristic: one node per 25 m² for basic coverage\n335\t\t// This is based on the Fresnel zone size (~5m radius per node)\n336\t\tminNodes := int(math.Ceil(area / 25.0))\n337\t\n338\t\t// At least 2 nodes needed for any localization\n339\t\tif minNodes < 2 {\n340\t\t\tminNodes = 2\n341\t\t}\n342\t\n343\t\t// For better GDOP (< 4), add more nodes\n344\t\tif targetGDOP < 4.0 {\n345\t\t\tminNodes = int(math.Ceil(float64(minNodes) * 1.5))\n346\t\t}\n347\t\n348\t\treturn
