Useful for quick initial placement.\nfunc CornerPositions(s *Space) []Point {\n\tminX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ := s.Bounds()\n\theight := (maxZ - minZ) / 2 // Average height\n\n\treturn []Point{\n\t\t{X: minX, Y: minY, Z: height},          // Bottom-left, high\n\t\t{X: maxX, Y: minY, Z: height},          // Bottom-right, high\n\t\t{X: minX, Y: maxY, Z: height},          // Top-left, high\n\t\t{X: maxX, Y: maxY, Z: height},          // Top-right, high\n\t\t{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: minY, Z: 0.3}, // Bottom-middle, low\n\t\t{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: maxY, Z: 0.3}, // Top-middle, low\n\t}\n}\n\n// SuggestedNodes creates a suggested node set for a space\n// with nodes positioned at corners and mid-points\nfunc SuggestedNodes(s *Space, count int) *NodeSet {\n\tns := NewNodeSet()\n\tpositions := CornerPositions(s)\n\n\t// Use corner positions, then add random positions if needed\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\tvar pos Point\n\t\tif i < len(positions) {\n\t\t\tpos = positions[i]\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Random position within bounds\n\t\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := s.Bounds()\n\t\t\tpos = Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: minX + rand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n\t\t\t\tY: minY + rand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n\t\t\t\tZ: rand.Float64() * maxZ,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trole := RoleTXRX\n\n\t\t// Last node can be AP for passive radar\n\t\tif i == count-1 {\n\t\t\tns.AddAPNode(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\t\"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\",\n\t\t\t\t6,\n\t\t\t\tpos,\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tns.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tpos,\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\tns.nodes[i].Role = role\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ns\n}\n\n// GenerateAllLinks creates all possible links between nodes in the set.\n// For TX/RX or TX_RX nodes, this creates bidirectional links.\n// For passive radar (AP nodes), creates links from AP to each RX node.\nfunc GenerateAllLinks(ns *NodeSet) []Link {\n\tenabled := ns.Enabled()\n\tlinks := make([]Link, 0)\n\n\tfor _, tx := range enabled {\n\t\tfor _, rx := range enabled {\n\t\t\t// Skip self-links\n\t\t\tif tx.ID == rx.ID {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Determine if this link should exist based on roles\n\t\t\tif shouldCreateLink(tx, rx) {\n\t\t\t\tlinks = append(links, Link{TX: tx, RX: rx})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn links\n}\n\n// shouldCreateLink determines if a link should be created between two nodes\n// based on their roles. Links are created when:\n// - TX node -> RX node\n// - TX_RX node -> any other node (bidirectional communication)\n// - AP node -> RX node (passive radar)\nfunc shouldCreateLink(tx, rx *Node) bool {\n\t// AP (passive radar TX) to RX/TX_RX/Passive\n\tif tx.IsAP() {\n\t\treturn rx.Role == RoleRX || rx.Role == RoleTXRX || rx.Role == RolePassive\n\t}\n\n\t// Regular TX to RX/TX_RX\n\tif tx.Role == RoleTX {\n\t\treturn rx.Role == RoleRX || rx.Role == RoleTXRX\n\t}\n\n\t// TX_RX can both TX and RX, so link to any RX/TX_RX\n\tif tx.Role == RoleTXRX {\n\t\treturn rx.Role == RoleRX || rx.Role == RoleTXRX\n\t}\n\n\t// RX nodes don't transmit\n\tif tx.Role == RoleRX {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Passive nodes don't transmit (unless they're also RX)\n\tif tx.Role == RolePassive {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// MinimumNodeCount estimates the minimum number of nodes needed for\n// reasonable coverage of the given space.\n// This is a heuristic based on space dimensions.\n// Note: The actual implementation is in gdop.go to avoid duplication.\nfunc MinimumNodeCountFromNode(s *Space, targetGDOP float64) int {\n\twidth, depth, _ := s.Dimensions()\n\tarea := width * depth\n\n\t// Heuristic: one node per 25 m² for basic coverage\n\t// This is based on the Fresnel zone size (~5m radius per node)\n\tminNodes := int(math.Ceil(area / 25.0))\n\n\t// At least 2 nodes needed for any localization\n\tif minNodes < 2 {\n\t\tminNodes = 2\n\t}\n\n\t// For better GDOP (< 4), add more nodes\n\tif targetGDOP < 4.0 {\n\t\tminNodes = int(math.Ceil(float64(minNodes) * 1.5))\n\t}\n\n\treturn minNodes\n}\n","numLines":350,"startLine":1,"totalLines":350}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_61c4dbaa3eb0491b9ed87ed1","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t// Package simulator provides signal propagation modeling for CSI simulation.\n2\tpackage simulator\n3\t\n4\timport (\n5\t\t\"math\"\n6\t)\n7\t\n8\t// PropagationModel computes expected CSI amplitude using a two-ray model\n9\t// (direct path + first-order reflections) with wall attenuation.\n10\ttype PropagationModel struct {\n11\t\tspace           *Space\n12\t\ttxPower         float64 // Transmit power in dBm (default -30)\n13\t\treflectionCoeff float64 // Reflection coefficient (power, dimensionless)\n14\t}\n15\t\n16\t// NewPropagationModel creates a new propagation model for the given space.\n17\tfunc NewPropagationModel(space *Space) *PropagationModel {\n18\t\treturn &PropagationModel{\n19\t\t\tspace:           space,\n20\t\t\ttxPower:         -30.0, // Default TX power\n21\t\t\treflectionCoeff: 0.3,   // Default reflection coefficient\n22\t\t}\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// SetTXPower sets the transmit power in dBm\n26\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) SetTXPower(power float64) {\n27\t\tpm.txPower = power\n28\t}\n29\t\n30\t// SetReflectionCoefficient sets the reflection coefficient (power ratio)\n31\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) SetReflectionCoefficient(coeff float64) {\n32\t\tpm.reflectionCoeff = coeff\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\t// RayResult represents the result of a ray propagation computation\n36\ttype RayResult struct {\n37\t\tPathLength    float64 // Total path length in meters\n38\t\tPower         float64 // Linear power (mW-equivalent)\n39\t\tPhase         float64 // Phase in radians\n40\t\tReflectionIdx int     // 0 = direct, 1+ = reflection index\n41\t}\n42\t\n43\t// AmplitudeAt computes the expected CSI amplitude at a walker position\n44\t// for a link between TX and RX nodes. This is the primary method used\n45\t// by the simulator to generate synthetic CSI data.\n46\t//\n47\t// The model uses a two-ray propagation model:\n48\t// 1. Direct path: TX -> walker -> RX (bistatic scattering)\n49\t// 2. First-order reflections: TX -> wall/floor/ceiling -> RX\n50\t//\n51\t// Returns a normalized amplitude value suitable for deltaRMS computation.\n52\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) AmplitudeAt(tx, rx, walker Point) float64 {\n53\t\t// Check if walker is in a valid Fresnel zone\n54\t\tzone := FresnelZoneNumber(tx, rx, walker)\n55\t\tif zone > 5 {\n56\t\t\t// Outside zone 5, no meaningful contribution\n57\t\t\treturn 0.0\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Compute signal with walker present (scattering model)\n61\t\tsignalWithWalker := pm.computeSignalStrength(tx, rx, walker)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Compute signal without walker (empty room baseline)\n64\t\tsignalEmptyRoom := pm.computeEmptyRoomSignal(tx, rx)\n65\t\n66\t\t// deltaRMS is the relative change\n67\t\t// deltaRMS = |amplitude(W) - amplitude(empty_room)| / amplitude(empty_room)\n68\t\tif signalEmptyRoom < 1e-9 {\n69\t\t\t// Avoid division by zero\n70\t\t\treturn signalWithWalker * 1000.0\n71\t\t}\n72\t\n73\t\tdeltaRMS := math.Abs(signalWithWalker-signalEmptyRoom) / signalEmptyRoom\n74\t\n75\t\t// Apply Fresnel zone modulation (zone 1 has highest effect)\n76\t\tzoneModulation := fresnelZoneModulation(zone)\n77\t\tdeltaRMS *= zoneModulation\n78\t\n79\t\t// Clamp to reasonable range [0, 0.3]\n80\t\tif deltaRMS < 0 {\n81\t\t\tdeltaRMS = 0\n82\t\t}\n83\t\tif deltaRMS > 0.3 {\n84\t\t\tdeltaRMS = 0.3\n85\t\t}\n86\t\n87\t\treturn deltaRMS\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\t// computeSignalStrength computes the received signal strength when a walker\n91\t// is present at the given position. Uses bistatic scattering model:\n92\t// - Direct path: TX -> walker -> RX\n93\t// - Reflected paths: TX -> wall -> RX\n94\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeSignalStrength(tx, rx, walker Point) float64 {\n95\t\t// Direct bistatic path: TX -> walker -> RX\n96\t\td1 := tx.Distance(walker)\n97\t\td2 := walker.Distance(rx)\n98\t\tdirectPathLen := d1 + d2\n99\t\n100\t\t// Path loss for direct path\n101\t\tdirectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(directPathLen)\n102\t\n103\t\t// Wall attenuation for TX->walker and walker->RX segments\n104\t\twallLoss := pm.wallAttenuation(tx, walker, pm.space)\n105\t\twallLoss += pm.wallAttenuation(walker, rx, pm.space)\n106\t\n107\t\t// Direct received power (dBm)\n108\t\tdirectPowerDBm := pm.txPower - directPathLoss - wallLoss\n109\t\n110\t\t// Convert to linear power\n111\t\tdirectPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (directPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n112\t\n113\t\t// Add first-order reflections\n114\t\treflectionPower := pm.computeReflectionPower(tx, rx)\n115\t\n116\t\t// Combine direct and reflected power (coherent sum approximation)\n117\t\t// In reality, these would interfere, but for simulation we use power addition\n118\t\ttotalPower := directPowerLinear + reflectionPower\n119\t\n120\t\treturn totalPower\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\t// computeEmptyRoomSignal computes the received signal strength in an empty room\n124\t// (no walker present). Uses TX -> RX direct path plus reflections.\n125\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeEmptyRoomSignal(tx, rx Point) float64 {\n126\t\t// Direct path length\n127\t\tdirectPathLen := tx.Distance(rx)\n128\t\n129\t\t// Path loss for direct path\n130\t\tdirectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(directPathLen)\n131\t\n132\t\t// Wall attenuation for direct TX->RX path\n133\t\twallLoss := pm.wallAttenuation(tx, rx, pm.space)\n134\t\n135\t\t// Direct received power (dBm)\n136\t\tdirectPowerDBm := pm.txPower - directPathLoss - wallLoss\n137\t\n138\t\t// Convert to linear power\n139\t\tdirectPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (directPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n140\t\n141\t\t// Add first-order reflections\n142\t\treflectionPower := pm.computeReflectionPower(tx, rx)\n143\t\n144\t\t// Combine direct and reflected power\n145\t\ttotalPower := directPowerLinear + reflectionPower\n146\t\n147\t\treturn totalPower\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\t// computeReflectionPower computes the total power from all first-order reflections.\n151\t// This includes wall reflections, floor reflections, and ceiling reflections.\n152\t// Only the strongest reflection per surface type is retained.\n153\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeReflectionPower(tx, rx Point) float64 {\n154\t\tmaxPower := 0.0\n155\t\n156\t\t// Try each wall as a potential reflector\n157\t\tfor _, wall := range pm.space.GetWalls() {\n158\t\t\treflectionPoint, valid := pm.computeWallReflectionPoint(tx, rx, wall)\n159\t\t\tif !valid {\n160\t\t\t\tcontinue\n161\t\t\t}\n162\t\n163\t\t\t// Compute reflected path length\n164\t\t\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(rx)\n165\t\n166\t\t\t// Compute path loss for reflected path\n167\t\t\treflectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n168\t\n169\t\t\t// Wall penetration loss (signal passes through wall to reflect, or reflects off surface)\n170\t\t\t// For reflection, we use the material's reflection coefficient\n171\t\t\t// Harder surfaces (metal, concrete) reflect more\n172\t\t\tmaterialReflectionCoeff := pm.materialReflectionCoefficient(wall.Material)\n173\t\n174\t\t\t// Reflected power\n175\t\t\t// P_refl = P_tx × R × PL(d_refl)\n176\t\t\treflectionPowerDBm := pm.txPower - reflectPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(materialReflectionCoeff)\n177\t\n178\t\t\t// Convert to linear\n179\t\t\treflectionPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (reflectionPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n180\t\n181\t\t\tif reflectionPowerLinear > maxPower {\n182\t\t\t\tmaxPower = reflectionPowerLinear\n183\t\t\t}\n184\t\t}\n185\t\n186\t\t// Floor reflection (Z = 0 plane)\n187\t\tif floorPower := pm.computeFloorReflectionPower(tx, rx); floorPower > maxPower {\n188\t\t\tmaxPower = floorPower\n189\t\t}\n190\t\n191\t\t// Ceiling reflection (Z = room height)\n192\t\tif ceilingPower := pm.computeCeilingReflectionPower(tx, rx); ceilingPower > maxPower {\n193\t\t\tmaxPower = ceilingPower\n194\t\t}\n195\t\n196\t\treturn maxPower\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t// materialReflectionCoefficient returns the reflection coefficient for a material.\n200\t// Higher values = more reflective (less absorption).\n201\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) materialReflectionCoefficient(material WallMaterial) float64 {\n202\t\t// Base reflection coefficient\n203\t\tbaseCoeff := pm.reflectionCoeff\n204\t\n205\t\t// Modify based on material properties\n206\t\tswitch material {\n207\t\tcase MaterialMetal:\n208\t\t\treturn 0.9 // Metal reflects most signal\n209\t\tcase MaterialGlass:\n210\t\t\treturn 0.7 // Glass is reflective\n211\t\tcase MaterialBrick, MaterialConcrete:\n212\t\t\treturn 0.5 // Brick/concrete are somewhat reflective\n213\t\tcase MaterialDrywall:\n214\t\t\treturn baseCoeff // Use default\n215\t\tdefault:\n216\t\t\treturn baseCoeff\n217\t\t}\n218\t}\n219\t\n220\t// computeWallReflectionPoint computes the specular reflection point on a wall segment\n221\t// for a ray from tx to rx. Returns the reflection point and a validity flag.\n222\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeWallReflectionPoint(tx, rx Point, wall WallSegment) (Point, bool) {\n223\t\t// For a vertical wall, we compute the 2D reflection point on the XY plane\n224\t\t// and then use the average Z height.\n225\t\n226\t\t// Project to 2D (ignore Z for wall reflection calculation)\n227\t\ttx2D := Point{X: tx.X, Y: tx.Y}\n228\t\trx2D := Point{X: rx.X, Y: rx.Y}\n229\t\twallP1 := Point{X: wall.P1.X, Y: wall.P1.Y}\n230\t\twallP2 := Point{X: wall.P2.X, Y: wall.P2.Y}\n231\t\n232\t\t// Compute specular reflection point using image source method\n233\t\t// For a line segment, find the point that minimizes total path length\n234\t\n235\t\t// Wall direction vector\n236\t\twallDirX := wallP2.X - wallP1.X\n237\t\twallDirY := wallP2.Y - wallP1.Y\n238\t\twallLen := math.Sqrt(wallDirX*wallDirX + wallDirY*wallDirY)\n239\t\tif wallLen < 0.01 {\n240\t\t\treturn Point{}, false // Wall too short\n241\t\t}\n242\t\n243\t\t// Normalize wall direction\n244\t\twallDirX /= wallLen\n245\t\twallDirY /= wallLen\n246\t\n247\t\t// Wall normal (perpendicular to wall direction)\n248\t\tnormalX := -wallDirY\n249\t\tnormalY := wallDirX\n250\t\n251\t\t// Check if TX and RX are on opposite sides of the wall\n252\t\t// Vector from wall point to TX\n253\t\ttxToWallX := tx2D.X - wallP1.X\n254\t\ttxToWallY := tx2D.Y - wallP1.Y\n255\t\n256\t\t// Vector from wall point to RX\n257\t\trxToWallX := rx2D.X - wallP1.X\n258\t\trxToWallY := rx2D.Y - wallP1.Y\n259\t\n260\t\t// Check if TX and RX are on same side of infinite line containing wall\n261\t\ttxSide := txToWallX*normalX + txToWallY*normalY\n262\t\trxSide := rxToWallX*normalX + rxToWallY*normalY\n263\t\n264\t\t// For valid reflection, TX and RX should be on same side\n265\t\t// (otherwise the signal would pass through the wall, not reflect)\n266\t\tif txSide*rxSide < 0 {\n267\t\t\t// Opposite sides - signal would pass through wall\n268\t\t\t// This is handled by wall attenuation, not reflection\n269\t\t\treturn Point{}, false\n270\t\t}\n271\t\n272\t\t// Compute reflection point using projection\n273\t\t// The reflection point is where the angle of incidence equals angle of reflection\n274\t\t// For a flat wall, this is the point on the wall that the ray would hit\n275\t\n276\t\t// Project TX onto wall line\n277\t\ttxProjX := wallP1.X\n278\t\ttxProjY := wallP1.Y\n279\t\ttxToWallDirX := tx2D.X - txProjX\n280\t\ttxToWallDirY := tx2D.Y - txProjY\n281\t\ttxProj := txToWallDirX*wallDirX + txToWallDirY*wallDirY\n282\t\n283\t\t// Compute reflection point parameter t along wall segment\n284\t\t// Using image source method: reflect RX across wall to get RX', then find intersection\n285\t\t// For simplicity, use midpoint projection\n286\t\tt := txProj\n287\t\n288\t\t// Clamp to segment\n289\t\tif t < 0 {\n290\t\t\tt = 0\n291\t\t} else if t > wallLen {\n292\t\t\tt = wallLen\n293\t\t}\n294\t\n295\t\treflectionX := wallP1.X + t*wallDirX\n296\t\treflectionY := wallP1.Y + t*wallDirY\n297\t\n298\t\t// Use average Z height for reflection point\n299\t\treflectionZ := (tx.Z + rx.Z) / 2\n300\t\n301\t\t// Check if reflection point is within wall height bounds\n302\t\twallMinZ := math.Min(wall.P1.Z, wall.P2.Z)\n303\t\twallMaxZ := math.Max(wall.P1.Z, wall.P2.Z) + wall.Height\n304\t\n305\t\tif reflectionZ < wallMinZ || reflectionZ > wallMaxZ {\n306\t\t\treturn Point{}, false // Reflection point outside wall height\n307\t\t}\n308\t\n309\t\treturn Point{X: reflectionX, Y: reflectionY, Z: reflectionZ}, true\n310\t}\n311\t\n312\t// computeFloorReflectionPower computes reflection power from the floor (Z=0 plane).\n313\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeFloorReflectionPower(tx, rx Point) float64 {\n314\t\t// Floor reflection point (mirror of average height across Z=0)\n315\t\tfloorZ := 0.0\n316\t\n317\t\t// Reflection point in XY is the midpoint\n318\t\treflectionX := (tx.X + rx.X) / 2\n319\t\treflectionY := (tx.Y + rx.Y) / 2\n320\t\n321\t\treflectionPoint := Point{X: reflectionX, Y: reflectionY, Z: floorZ}\n322\t\n323\t\t// Compute reflected path length\n324\t\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(rx)\n325\t\n326\t\t// Path loss for reflected path\n327\t\treflectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n328\t\n329\t\t// Floor reflection coefficient (concrete/floor)\n330\t\tfloorCoeff := 0.4 // Typical floor reflection\n331\t\n332\t\t// Reflected power\n333\t\treflectionPowerDBm := pm.txPower - reflectPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(floorCoeff)\n334\t\treflectionPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (reflectionPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n335\t\n336\t\treturn reflectionPowerLinear\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\t// computeCeilingReflectionPower computes reflection power from the ceiling.\n340\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeCeilingReflectionPower(tx, rx Point) float64 {\n341\t\t// Get ceiling height from space bounds\n342\t\t_, _, _, _, _, maxZ := pm.space.Bounds()\n343\t\tceilingZ := maxZ\n344\t\n345\t\t// Reflection point in XY is the midpoint\n346\t\treflectionX := (tx.X + rx.X) / 2\n347\t\treflectionY := (tx.Y + rx.Y) / 2\n348\t\n349\t\treflectionPoint := Point{X: reflectionX, Y: reflectionY, Z: ceilingZ}\n350\t\n351\t\t// Compute reflected path length\n352\t\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(rx)\n353\t\n354\t\t// Path loss for reflected path\n355\t\treflectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n356\t\n357\t\t// Ceiling reflection coefficient (drywall/ceiling)\n358\t\tceilingCoeff := 0.3 // Typical ceiling reflection\n359\t\n360\t\t// Reflected power\n361\t\treflectionPowerDBm := pm.txPower - reflectPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(ceilingCoeff)\n362\t\treflectionPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (reflectionPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n363\t\n364\t\treturn reflectionPowerLinear\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// pathLoss computes path loss in dB using log-distance model.\n368\t// PL(d) = PL_0 + 10*n*log10(d/d_0)\n369\t// where PL_0 = 40 dB at d_0 = 1m, n = 2.0 (free space)\n370\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) pathLoss(distance float64) float64 {\n371\t\tconst PL0 = 40.0 // dB at 1m reference\n372\t\tconst d0 = 1.0   // reference distance in meters\n373\t\tconst n = 2.0    // path loss exponent (free space)\n374\t\n375\t\tif distance < 0.01 {\n376\t\t\tdistance = 0.01 // Avoid log(0)\n377\t\t}\n378\t\n379\t\treturn PL0 + 10*n*math.Log10(distance/d0)\n380\t}\n381\t\n382\t// wallAttenuation computes total wall attenuation for a path.\n383\t// Returns dB loss from walls intersecting the path.\n384\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) wallAttenuation(from, to Point, space *Space) float64 {\n385\t\ttotalLoss := 0.0\n386\t\n387\t\t// Check all walls in the space\n388\t\tfor _, wall := range space.GetWalls() {\n389\t\t\tif wall.IntersectsLine(from, to) {\n390\t\t\t\ttotalLoss += WallPenetrationLoss(wall.Material)\n391\t\t\t}\n392\t\t}\n393\t\n394\t\treturn totalLoss\n395\t}\n396\t\n397\t// fresnelZoneModulation returns the sensitivity modulation factor for a Fresnel zone.\n398\t// Zone 1 has maximum sensitivity (1.0), zone 5 has minimum (0.04).\n399\tfunc fresnelZoneModulation(zone int) float64 {\n400\t\tif zone < 1 {\n401\t\t\tzone = 1\n402\t\t}\n403\t\t// Zone decay: 1/zone^2 gives 1.0, 0.25, 0.11, 0.0625, 0.04 for zones 1-5\n404\t\treturn 1.0 / math.Pow(float64(zone), 2.0)\n405\t}\n406\t\n407\t// ComputeLinkActivity computes the expected deltaRMS for a link when a walker\n408\t// is at the given position. This is used by the simulation engine to determine\n409\t// which links are \"active\" (above threshold) during each tick.\n410\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) ComputeLinkActivity(link Link, walkerPos Point, threshold float64) float64 {\n411\t\tamplitude := pm.AmplitudeAt(link.TX.Position, link.RX.Position, walkerPos)\n412\t\treturn amplitude\n413\t}\n414\t\n415\t// ExpectedRSSI computes the expected RSSI in dBm for a receiver at the given distance\n416\t// from the transmitter, accounting for path loss and wall attenuation.\n417\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) ExpectedRSSI(tx, rx Point) int8 {\n418\t\tdistance := tx.Distance(rx)\n419\t\tpathLoss := pm.pathLoss(distance)\n420\t\twallLoss := pm.wallAttenuation(tx, rx, pm.space)\n421\t\n422\t\trssi := pm.txPower - pathLoss - wallLoss\n423\t\n424\t\t// Clamp to realistic range\n425\t\tif rssi < -90 {\n426\t\t\trssi = -90\n427\t\t}\n428\t\tif rssi > -30 {\n429\t\t\trssi = -30\n430\t\t}\n431\t\n432\t\treturn int8(rssi)\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// PhaseAtSubcarrier computes the expected phase at a given subcarrier index\n436\t// for a signal traveling from tx to walker to rx.\n437\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) PhaseAtSubcarrier(tx, rx, walker Point, subcarrierIndex int, frameNum int) float64 {\n438\t\t// Total path length (TX -> walker -> RX)\n439\t\td1 := tx.Distance(walker)\n440\t\td2 := walker.Distance(rx)\n441\t\ttotalDist := d1 + d2\n442\t\n443\t\t// Phase = 2π × k × Δf × (d / c)\n444\t\tphase := 2 * math.Pi * float64(subcarrierIndex) * SubcarrierSpacing * (totalDist / C)\n445\t\n446\t\t// Add small temporal variation for realism\n447\t\ttemporalPhase := 0.1 * math.Sin(2 * math.Pi * float64(frameNum) / 100.0)\n448\t\tphase += temporalPhase\n449\t\n450\t\t// Normalize to [-π, π]\n451\t\tfor phase > math.Pi {\n452\t\t\tphase -= 2 * math.Pi\n453\t\t}\n454\t\tfor phase < -math.Pi {\n455\t\t\tphase += 2 * math.Pi\n456\t\t}\n457\t\n458\t\treturn phase\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\t// ComputeRays computes all ray paths between TX and RX for detailed analysis.\n462\t// Returns direct ray plus first-order reflections (walls, floor, ceiling).\n463\tfunc (pm *PropagationModel) ComputeRays(tx, rx Point) []RayResult {\n464\t\trays := make([]RayResult, 0, 1+len(pm.space.GetWalls())+2)\n465\t\n466\t\t// Direct ray\n467\t\tdirectDist := tx.Distance(rx)\n468\t\tdirectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(directDist)\n469\t\tdirectWallLoss := pm.wallAttenuation(tx, rx, pm.space)\n470\t\tdirectPowerDBm := pm.txPower - directPathLoss - directWallLoss\n471\t\tdirectPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (directPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n472\t\tdirectPhase := 2 * math.Pi * directDist / Wavelength\n473\t\n474\t\trays = append(rays, RayResult{\n475\t\t\tPathLength:    directDist,\n476\t\t\tPower:         directPowerLinear,\n477\t\t\tPhase:         directPhase,\n478\t\t\tReflectionIdx: 0, // Direct path\n479\t\t})\n480\t\n481\t\t// Wall reflections\n482\t\tfor _, wall := range pm.space.GetWalls() {\n483\t\t\treflectionPoint, valid := pm.computeWallReflectionPoint(tx, rx, wall)\n484\t\t\tif !valid {\n485\t\t\t\tcontinue\n486\t\t\t}\n487\t\n488\t\t\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(rx)\n489\t\t\treflectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n490\t\t\tmaterialCoeff := pm.materialReflectionCoefficient(wall.Material)\n491\t\t\treflectionPowerDBm := pm.txPower - reflectPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(materialCoeff)\n492\t\t\treflectionPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (reflectionPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n493\t\t\treflectionPhase := 2 * math.Pi * dReflect / Wavelength\n494\t\n495\t\t\trays = append(rays, RayResult{\n496\t\t\t\tPathLength:    dReflect,\n497\t\t\t\tPower:         reflectionPowerLinear,\n498\t\t\t\tPhase:         reflectionPhase,\n499\t\t\t\tReflectionIdx: len(rays),\n500\t\t\t})\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\t// Floor reflection\n504\t\t_, _, _, _, _, maxZ := pm.space.Bounds()\n505\t\tfloorZ := 0.0\n506\t\treflectionZ := -((tx.Z + rx.Z) / 2)\n507\t\treflectionPoint := Point{X: (tx.X + rx.X) / 2, Y: (tx.Y + rx.Y) / 2, Z: floorZ}\n508\t\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(Point{X: reflectionPoint.X, Y: reflectionPoint.Y, Z: reflectionZ})\n509\t\tfloorCoeff := 0.4\n510\t\tfloorPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n511\t\tfloorPowerDBm := pm.txPower - floorPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(floorCoeff)\n512\t\tfloorPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (floorPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n513\t\tfloorPhase := 2 * math.Pi * dReflect / Wavelength\n514\t\n515\t\trays = append(rays, RayResult{\n516\t\t\tPathLength:    dReflect,\n517\t\t\tPower:         floorPowerLinear,\n518\t\t\tPhase:         floorPhase,\n519\t\t\tReflectionIdx: len(rays),\n520\t\t})\n521\t\n522\t\t// Ceiling reflection\n523\t\tceilingZ := maxZ\n524\t\treflectionZ = ceilingZ + (ceilingZ - (tx.Z+rx.Z)/2)\n525\t\treflectionPoint = Point{X: (tx.X + rx.X) / 2, Y: (tx.Y + rx.Y) / 2, Z: ceilingZ}\n526\t\tdReflect = tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(Point{X: reflectionPoint.X, Y: reflectionPoint.Y, Z: reflectionZ})\n527\t\tceilingCoeff := 0.3\n528\t\tceilingPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n529\t\tceilingPowerDBm := pm.txPower - ceilingPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(ceilingCoeff)\n530\t\tceilingPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (ceilingPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n531\t\tceilingPhase := 2 * math.Pi * dReflect / Wavelength\n532\t\n533\t\trays = append(rays, RayResult{\n534\t\t\tPathLength:    dReflect,\n535\t\t\tPower:         ceilingPowerLinear,\n536\t\t\tPhase:         ceilingPhase,\n537\t\t\tReflectionIdx: len(rays),\n538\t\t})\n539\t\n540\t\treturn rays\n541\t}\n542\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b3b417b-ffca-4e51-853f-9cbdc68e2df1","uuid":"acf17311-39a2-4976-9594-88c19327a8b1","timestamp":"2026-05-06T03:27:37.964Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/simulator/propagation.go","content":"// Package simulator provides signal propagation modeling for CSI simulation.\npackage simulator\n\nimport (\n\t\"math\"\n)\n\n// PropagationModel computes expected CSI amplitude using a two-ray model\n// (direct path + first-order reflections) with wall attenuation.\ntype PropagationModel struct {\n\tspace           *Space\n\ttxPower         float64 // Transmit power in dBm (default -30)\n\treflectionCoeff float64 // Reflection coefficient (power, dimensionless)\n}\n\n// NewPropagationModel creates a new propagation model for the given space.\nfunc NewPropagationModel(space *Space) *PropagationModel {\n\treturn &PropagationModel{\n\t\tspace:           space,\n\t\ttxPower:         -30.0, // Default TX power\n\t\treflectionCoeff: 0.3,   // Default reflection coefficient\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetTXPower sets the transmit power in dBm\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) SetTXPower(power float64) {\n\tpm.txPower = power\n}\n\n// SetReflectionCoefficient sets the reflection coefficient (power ratio)\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) SetReflectionCoefficient(coeff float64) {\n\tpm.reflectionCoeff = coeff\n}\n\n// RayResult represents the result of a ray propagation computation\ntype RayResult struct {\n\tPathLength    float64 // Total path length in meters\n\tPower         float64 // Linear power (mW-equivalent)\n\tPhase         float64 // Phase in radians\n\tReflectionIdx int     // 0 = direct, 1+ = reflection index\n}\n\n// AmplitudeAt computes the expected CSI amplitude at a walker position\n// for a link between TX and RX nodes. This is the primary method used\n// by the simulator to generate synthetic CSI data.\n//\n// The model uses a two-ray propagation model:\n// 1. Direct path: TX -> walker -> RX (bistatic scattering)\n// 2. First-order reflections: TX -> wall/floor/ceiling -> RX\n//\n// Returns a normalized amplitude value suitable for deltaRMS computation.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) AmplitudeAt(tx, rx, walker Point) float64 {\n\t// Check if walker is in a valid Fresnel zone\n\tzone := FresnelZoneNumber(tx, rx, walker)\n\tif zone > 5 {\n\t\t// Outside zone 5, no meaningful contribution\n\t\treturn 0.0\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute signal with walker present (scattering model)\n\tsignalWithWalker := pm.computeSignalStrength(tx, rx, walker)\n\n\t// Compute signal without walker (empty room baseline)\n\tsignalEmptyRoom := pm.computeEmptyRoomSignal(tx, rx)\n\n\t// deltaRMS is the relative change\n\t// deltaRMS = |amplitude(W) - amplitude(empty_room)| / amplitude(empty_room)\n\tif signalEmptyRoom < 1e-9 {\n\t\t// Avoid division by zero\n\t\treturn signalWithWalker * 1000.0\n\t}\n\n\tdeltaRMS := math.Abs(signalWithWalker-signalEmptyRoom) / signalEmptyRoom\n\n\t// Apply Fresnel zone modulation (zone 1 has highest effect)\n\tzoneModulation := fresnelZoneModulation(zone)\n\tdeltaRMS *= zoneModulation\n\n\t// Clamp to reasonable range [0, 0.3]\n\tif deltaRMS < 0 {\n\t\tdeltaRMS = 0\n\t}\n\tif deltaRMS > 0.3 {\n\t\tdeltaRMS = 0.3\n\t}\n\n\treturn deltaRMS\n}\n\n// computeSignalStrength computes the received signal strength when a walker\n// is present at the given position. Uses bistatic scattering model:\n// - Direct path: TX -> walker -> RX\n// - Reflected paths: TX -> wall -> RX\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeSignalStrength(tx, rx, walker Point) float64 {\n\t// Direct bistatic path: TX -> walker -> RX\n\td1 := tx.Distance(walker)\n\td2 := walker.Distance(rx)\n\tdirectPathLen := d1 + d2\n\n\t// Path loss for direct path\n\tdirectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(directPathLen)\n\n\t// Wall attenuation for TX->walker and walker->RX segments\n\twallLoss := pm.wallAttenuation(tx, walker, pm.space)\n\twallLoss += pm.wallAttenuation(walker, rx, pm.space)\n\n\t// Direct received power (dBm)\n\tdirectPowerDBm := pm.txPower - directPathLoss - wallLoss\n\n\t// Convert to linear power\n\tdirectPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (directPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\n\t// Add first-order reflections\n\treflectionPower := pm.computeReflectionPower(tx, rx)\n\n\t// Combine direct and reflected power (coherent sum approximation)\n\t// In reality, these would interfere, but for simulation we use power addition\n\ttotalPower := directPowerLinear + reflectionPower\n\n\treturn totalPower\n}\n\n// computeEmptyRoomSignal computes the received signal strength in an empty room\n// (no walker present). Uses TX -> RX direct path plus reflections.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeEmptyRoomSignal(tx, rx Point) float64 {\n\t// Direct path length\n\tdirectPathLen := tx.Distance(rx)\n\n\t// Path loss for direct path\n\tdirectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(directPathLen)\n\n\t// Wall attenuation for direct TX->RX path\n\twallLoss := pm.wallAttenuation(tx, rx, pm.space)\n\n\t// Direct received power (dBm)\n\tdirectPowerDBm := pm.txPower - directPathLoss - wallLoss\n\n\t// Convert to linear power\n\tdirectPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (directPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\n\t// Add first-order reflections\n\treflectionPower := pm.computeReflectionPower(tx, rx)\n\n\t// Combine direct and reflected power\n\ttotalPower := directPowerLinear + reflectionPower\n\n\treturn totalPower\n}\n\n// computeReflectionPower computes the total power from all first-order reflections.\n// This includes wall reflections, floor reflections, and ceiling reflections.\n// Only the strongest reflection per surface type is retained.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeReflectionPower(tx, rx Point) float64 {\n\tmaxPower := 0.0\n\n\t// Try each wall as a potential reflector\n\tfor _, wall := range pm.space.GetWalls() {\n\t\treflectionPoint, valid := pm.computeWallReflectionPoint(tx, rx, wall)\n\t\tif !valid {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Compute reflected path length\n\t\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(rx)\n\n\t\t// Compute path loss for reflected path\n\t\treflectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n\n\t\t// Wall penetration loss (signal passes through wall to reflect, or reflects off surface)\n\t\t// For reflection, we use the material's reflection coefficient\n\t\t// Harder surfaces (metal, concrete) reflect more\n\t\tmaterialReflectionCoeff := pm.materialReflectionCoefficient(wall.Material)\n\n\t\t// Reflected power\n\t\t// P_refl = P_tx × R × PL(d_refl)\n\t\treflectionPowerDBm := pm.txPower - reflectPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(materialReflectionCoeff)\n\n\t\t// Convert to linear\n\t\treflectionPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (reflectionPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\n\t\tif reflectionPowerLinear > maxPower {\n\t\t\tmaxPower = reflectionPowerLinear\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Floor reflection (Z = 0 plane)\n\tif floorPower := pm.computeFloorReflectionPower(tx, rx); floorPower > maxPower {\n\t\tmaxPower = floorPower\n\t}\n\n\t// Ceiling reflection (Z = room height)\n\tif ceilingPower := pm.computeCeilingReflectionPower(tx, rx); ceilingPower > maxPower {\n\t\tmaxPower = ceilingPower\n\t}\n\n\treturn maxPower\n}\n\n// materialReflectionCoefficient returns the reflection coefficient for a material.\n// Higher values = more reflective (less absorption).\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) materialReflectionCoefficient(material WallMaterial) float64 {\n\t// Base reflection coefficient\n\tbaseCoeff := pm.reflectionCoeff\n\n\t// Modify based on material properties\n\tswitch material {\n\tcase MaterialMetal:\n\t\treturn 0.9 // Metal reflects most signal\n\tcase MaterialGlass:\n\t\treturn 0.7 // Glass is reflective\n\tcase MaterialBrick, MaterialConcrete:\n\t\treturn 0.5 // Brick/concrete are somewhat reflective\n\tcase MaterialDrywall:\n\t\treturn baseCoeff // Use default\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn baseCoeff\n\t}\n}\n\n// computeWallReflectionPoint computes the specular reflection point on a wall segment\n// for a ray from tx to rx. Returns the reflection point and a validity flag.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeWallReflectionPoint(tx, rx Point, wall WallSegment) (Point, bool) {\n\t// For a vertical wall, we compute the 2D reflection point on the XY plane\n\t// and then use the average Z height.\n\n\t// Project to 2D (ignore Z for wall reflection calculation)\n\ttx2D := Point{X: tx.X, Y: tx.Y}\n\trx2D := Point{X: rx.X, Y: rx.Y}\n\twallP1 := Point{X: wall.P1.X, Y: wall.P1.Y}\n\twallP2 := Point{X: wall.P2.X, Y: wall.P2.Y}\n\n\t// Compute specular reflection point using image source method\n\t// For a line segment, find the point that minimizes total path length\n\n\t// Wall direction vector\n\twallDirX := wallP2.X - wallP1.X\n\twallDirY := wallP2.Y - wallP1.Y\n\twallLen := math.Sqrt(wallDirX*wallDirX + wallDirY*wallDirY)\n\tif wallLen < 0.01 {\n\t\treturn Point{}, false // Wall too short\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize wall direction\n\twallDirX /= wallLen\n\twallDirY /= wallLen\n\n\t// Wall normal (perpendicular to wall direction)\n\tnormalX := -wallDirY\n\tnormalY := wallDirX\n\n\t// Check if TX and RX are on opposite sides of the wall\n\t// Vector from wall point to TX\n\ttxToWallX := tx2D.X - wallP1.X\n\ttxToWallY := tx2D.Y - wallP1.Y\n\n\t// Vector from wall point to RX\n\trxToWallX := rx2D.X - wallP1.X\n\trxToWallY := rx2D.Y - wallP1.Y\n\n\t// Check if TX and RX are on same side of infinite line containing wall\n\ttxSide := txToWallX*normalX + txToWallY*normalY\n\trxSide := rxToWallX*normalX + rxToWallY*normalY\n\n\t// For valid reflection, TX and RX should be on same side\n\t// (otherwise the signal would pass through the wall, not reflect)\n\tif txSide*rxSide < 0 {\n\t\t// Opposite sides - signal would pass through wall\n\t\t// This is handled by wall attenuation, not reflection\n\t\treturn Point{}, false\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute reflection point using projection\n\t// The reflection point is where the angle of incidence equals angle of reflection\n\t// For a flat wall, this is the point on the wall that the ray would hit\n\n\t// Project TX onto wall line\n\ttxProjX := wallP1.X\n\ttxProjY := wallP1.Y\n\ttxToWallDirX := tx2D.X - txProjX\n\ttxToWallDirY := tx2D.Y - txProjY\n\ttxProj := txToWallDirX*wallDirX + txToWallDirY*wallDirY\n\n\t// Compute reflection point parameter t along wall segment\n\t// Using image source method: reflect RX across wall to get RX', then find intersection\n\t// For simplicity, use midpoint projection\n\tt := txProj\n\n\t// Clamp to segment\n\tif t < 0 {\n\t\tt = 0\n\t} else if t > wallLen {\n\t\tt = wallLen\n\t}\n\n\treflectionX := wallP1.X + t*wallDirX\n\treflectionY := wallP1.Y + t*wallDirY\n\n\t// Use average Z height for reflection point\n\treflectionZ := (tx.Z + rx.Z) / 2\n\n\t// Check if reflection point is within wall height bounds\n\twallMinZ := math.Min(wall.P1.Z, wall.P2.Z)\n\twallMaxZ := math.Max(wall.P1.Z, wall.P2.Z) + wall.Height\n\n\tif reflectionZ < wallMinZ || reflectionZ > wallMaxZ {\n\t\treturn Point{}, false // Reflection point outside wall height\n\t}\n\n\treturn Point{X: reflectionX, Y: reflectionY, Z: reflectionZ}, true\n}\n\n// computeFloorReflectionPower computes reflection power from the floor (Z=0 plane).\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeFloorReflectionPower(tx, rx Point) float64 {\n\t// Floor reflection point (mirror of average height across Z=0)\n\tfloorZ := 0.0\n\n\t// Reflection point in XY is the midpoint\n\treflectionX := (tx.X + rx.X) / 2\n\treflectionY := (tx.Y + rx.Y) / 2\n\n\treflectionPoint := Point{X: reflectionX, Y: reflectionY, Z: floorZ}\n\n\t// Compute reflected path length\n\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(rx)\n\n\t// Path loss for reflected path\n\treflectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n\n\t// Floor reflection coefficient (concrete/floor)\n\tfloorCoeff := 0.4 // Typical floor reflection\n\n\t// Reflected power\n\treflectionPowerDBm := pm.txPower - reflectPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(floorCoeff)\n\treflectionPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (reflectionPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\n\treturn reflectionPowerLinear\n}\n\n// computeCeilingReflectionPower computes reflection power from the ceiling.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) computeCeilingReflectionPower(tx, rx Point) float64 {\n\t// Get ceiling height from space bounds\n\t_, _, _, _, _, maxZ := pm.space.Bounds()\n\tceilingZ := maxZ\n\n\t// Reflection point in XY is the midpoint\n\treflectionX := (tx.X + rx.X) / 2\n\treflectionY := (tx.Y + rx.Y) / 2\n\n\treflectionPoint := Point{X: reflectionX, Y: reflectionY, Z: ceilingZ}\n\n\t// Compute reflected path length\n\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(rx)\n\n\t// Path loss for reflected path\n\treflectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n\n\t// Ceiling reflection coefficient (drywall/ceiling)\n\tceilingCoeff := 0.3 // Typical ceiling reflection\n\n\t// Reflected power\n\treflectionPowerDBm := pm.txPower - reflectPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(ceilingCoeff)\n\treflectionPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (reflectionPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\n\treturn reflectionPowerLinear\n}\n\n// pathLoss computes path loss in dB using log-distance model.\n// PL(d) = PL_0 + 10*n*log10(d/d_0)\n// where PL_0 = 40 dB at d_0 = 1m, n = 2.0 (free space)\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) pathLoss(distance float64) float64 {\n\tconst PL0 = 40.0 // dB at 1m reference\n\tconst d0 = 1.0   // reference distance in meters\n\tconst n = 2.0    // path loss exponent (free space)\n\n\tif distance < 0.01 {\n\t\tdistance = 0.01 // Avoid log(0)\n\t}\n\n\treturn PL0 + 10*n*math.Log10(distance/d0)\n}\n\n// wallAttenuation computes total wall attenuation for a path.\n// Returns dB loss from walls intersecting the path.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) wallAttenuation(from, to Point, space *Space) float64 {\n\ttotalLoss := 0.0\n\n\t// Check all walls in the space\n\tfor _, wall := range space.GetWalls() {\n\t\tif wall.IntersectsLine(from, to) {\n\t\t\ttotalLoss += WallPenetrationLoss(wall.Material)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn totalLoss\n}\n\n// fresnelZoneModulation returns the sensitivity modulation factor for a Fresnel zone.\n// Zone 1 has maximum sensitivity (1.0), zone 5 has minimum (0.04).\nfunc fresnelZoneModulation(zone int) float64 {\n\tif zone < 1 {\n\t\tzone = 1\n\t}\n\t// Zone decay: 1/zone^2 gives 1.0, 0.25, 0.11, 0.0625, 0.04 for zones 1-5\n\treturn 1.0 / math.Pow(float64(zone), 2.0)\n}\n\n// ComputeLinkActivity computes the expected deltaRMS for a link when a walker\n// is at the given position. This is used by the simulation engine to determine\n// which links are \"active\" (above threshold) during each tick.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) ComputeLinkActivity(link Link, walkerPos Point, threshold float64) float64 {\n\tamplitude := pm.AmplitudeAt(link.TX.Position, link.RX.Position, walkerPos)\n\treturn amplitude\n}\n\n// ExpectedRSSI computes the expected RSSI in dBm for a receiver at the given distance\n// from the transmitter, accounting for path loss and wall attenuation.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) ExpectedRSSI(tx, rx Point) int8 {\n\tdistance := tx.Distance(rx)\n\tpathLoss := pm.pathLoss(distance)\n\twallLoss := pm.wallAttenuation(tx, rx, pm.space)\n\n\trssi := pm.txPower - pathLoss - wallLoss\n\n\t// Clamp to realistic range\n\tif rssi < -90 {\n\t\trssi = -90\n\t}\n\tif rssi > -30 {\n\t\trssi = -30\n\t}\n\n\treturn int8(rssi)\n}\n\n// PhaseAtSubcarrier computes the expected phase at a given subcarrier index\n// for a signal traveling from tx to walker to rx.\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) PhaseAtSubcarrier(tx, rx, walker Point, subcarrierIndex int, frameNum int) float64 {\n\t// Total path length (TX -> walker -> RX)\n\td1 := tx.Distance(walker)\n\td2 := walker.Distance(rx)\n\ttotalDist := d1 + d2\n\n\t// Phase = 2π × k × Δf × (d / c)\n\tphase := 2 * math.Pi * float64(subcarrierIndex) * SubcarrierSpacing * (totalDist / C)\n\n\t// Add small temporal variation for realism\n\ttemporalPhase := 0.1 * math.Sin(2 * math.Pi * float64(frameNum) / 100.0)\n\tphase += temporalPhase\n\n\t// Normalize to [-π, π]\n\tfor phase > math.Pi {\n\t\tphase -= 2 * math.Pi\n\t}\n\tfor phase < -math.Pi {\n\t\tphase += 2 * math.Pi\n\t}\n\n\treturn phase\n}\n\n// ComputeRays computes all ray paths between TX and RX for detailed analysis.\n// Returns direct ray plus first-order reflections (walls, floor, ceiling).\nfunc (pm *PropagationModel) ComputeRays(tx, rx Point) []RayResult {\n\trays := make([]RayResult, 0, 1+len(pm.space.GetWalls())+2)\n\n\t// Direct ray\n\tdirectDist := tx.Distance(rx)\n\tdirectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(directDist)\n\tdirectWallLoss := pm.wallAttenuation(tx, rx, pm.space)\n\tdirectPowerDBm := pm.txPower - directPathLoss - directWallLoss\n\tdirectPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (directPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\tdirectPhase := 2 * math.Pi * directDist / Wavelength\n\n\trays = append(rays, RayResult{\n\t\tPathLength:    directDist,\n\t\tPower:         directPowerLinear,\n\t\tPhase:         directPhase,\n\t\tReflectionIdx: 0, // Direct path\n\t})\n\n\t// Wall reflections\n\tfor _, wall := range pm.space.GetWalls() {\n\t\treflectionPoint, valid := pm.computeWallReflectionPoint(tx, rx, wall)\n\t\tif !valid {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(rx)\n\t\treflectPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n\t\tmaterialCoeff := pm.materialReflectionCoefficient(wall.Material)\n\t\treflectionPowerDBm := pm.txPower - reflectPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(materialCoeff)\n\t\treflectionPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (reflectionPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\t\treflectionPhase := 2 * math.Pi * dReflect / Wavelength\n\n\t\trays = append(rays, RayResult{\n\t\t\tPathLength:    dReflect,\n\t\t\tPower:         reflectionPowerLinear,\n\t\t\tPhase:         reflectionPhase,\n\t\t\tReflectionIdx: len(rays),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Floor reflection\n\t_, _, _, _, _, maxZ := pm.space.Bounds()\n\tfloorZ := 0.0\n\treflectionZ := -((tx.Z + rx.Z) / 2)\n\treflectionPoint := Point{X: (tx.X + rx.X) / 2, Y: (tx.Y + rx.Y) / 2, Z: floorZ}\n\tdReflect := tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(Point{X: reflectionPoint.X, Y: reflectionPoint.Y, Z: reflectionZ})\n\tfloorCoeff := 0.4\n\tfloorPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n\tfloorPowerDBm := pm.txPower - floorPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(floorCoeff)\n\tfloorPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (floorPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\tfloorPhase := 2 * math.Pi * dReflect / Wavelength\n\n\trays = append(rays, RayResult{\n\t\tPathLength:    dReflect,\n\t\tPower:         floorPowerLinear,\n\t\tPhase:         floorPhase,\n\t\tReflectionIdx: len(rays),\n\t})\n\n\t// Ceiling reflection\n\tceilingZ := maxZ\n\treflectionZ = ceilingZ + (ceilingZ - (tx.Z+rx.Z)/2)\n\treflectionPoint = Point{X: (tx.X + rx.X) / 2, Y: (tx.Y + rx.Y) / 2, Z: ceilingZ}\n\tdReflect = tx.Distance(reflectionPoint) + reflectionPoint.Distance(Point{X: reflectionPoint.X, Y: reflectionPoint.Y, Z: reflectionZ})\n\tceilingCoeff := 0.3\n\tceilingPathLoss := pm.pathLoss(dReflect)\n\tceilingPowerDBm := pm.txPower - ceilingPathLoss + 10*math.Log10(ceilingCoeff)\n\tceilingPowerLinear := math.Pow(10.0, (ceilingPowerDBm+30.0)/10.0)\n\tceilingPhase := 2 * math.Pi * dReflect / Wavelength\n\n\trays = append(rays, RayResult{\n\t\tPathLength:    dReflect,\n\t\tPower:         ceilingPowerLinear,\n\t\tPhase:         ceilingPhase,\n\t\tReflectionIdx: len(rays),\n\t})\n\n\treturn rays\n}\n","numLines":542,"startLine":1,"totalLines":542}}}

append(recs, Recommendation{\n172\t\t\t\tPriority: \"high\",\n173\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Coverage is below 50%% (%.0f%%). Consider adding more nodes.\", coverageScore),\n174\t\t\t\tImpact:   0.3,\n175\t\t\t})\n176\t\t}\n177\t\n178\t\t// Check node count\n179\t\tnodeCount := nodes.Count()\n180\t\tif nodeCount < 4 {\n181\t\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n182\t\t\t\tPriority: \"medium\",\n183\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Only %d nodes. For best accuracy, use at least 4 nodes.\", nodeCount),\n184\t\t\t\tImpact:   0.2,\n185\t\t\t})\n186\t\t}\n187\t\n188\t\t// Check height diversity\n189\t\thasLow, hasHigh := false, false\n190\t\tfor _, node := range nodes.All() {\n191\t\t\tif node.Position.Z < 1.0 {\n192\t\t\t\thasLow = true\n193\t\t\t}\n194\t\t\tif node.Position.Z > 2.0 {\n195\t\t\t\thasHigh = true\n196\t\t\t}\n197\t\t}\n198\t\n199\t\tif !hasLow || !hasHigh {\n200\t\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n201\t\t\t\tPriority: \"medium\",\n202\t\t\t\tMessage:  \"For better Z-axis accuracy, place nodes at mixed heights (some low, some high).\",\n203\t\t\t\tImpact:   0.15,\n204\t\t\t})\n205\t\t}\n206\t\n207\t\t// Find worst coverage areas\n208\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n209\t\tif len(gdopMap) > 0 {\n210\t\t\t// Find cells with worst GDOP (highest values, excluding infinity)\n211\t\t\tmaxGDOP := 0.0\n212\t\t\tworstIdx := -1\n213\t\n214\t\t\tfor i, gdop := range gdopMap {\n215\t\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(gdop, 0) && gdop > maxGDOP {\n216\t\t\t\t\tmaxGDOP = gdop\n217\t\t\t\t\tworstIdx = i\n218\t\t\t\t}\n219\t\t\t}\n220\t\n221\t\t\tif maxGDOP > 8.0 && worstIdx >= 0 {\n222\t\t\t\t// Compute position from index\n223\t\t\t\twidthCells := int(math.Ceil((maxX - minX) / 0.2))\n224\t\t\t\tdepthCells := int(math.Ceil((maxY - minY) / 0.2))\n225\t\n226\t\t\t\t_ = worstIdx / (widthCells * depthCells) // z-layer index, not used in 2D recommendation\n227\t\t\t\tremainder := worstIdx % (widthCells * depthCells)\n228\t\t\t\tx := remainder / depthCells\n229\t\t\t\ty := remainder % depthCells\n230\t\n231\t\t\t\tposX := minX + float64(x)*0.2 + 0.1\n232\t\t\t\tposY := minY + float64(y)*0.2 + 0.1\n233\t\n234\t\t\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n235\t\t\t\t\tPriority: \"high\",\n236\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Poor coverage detected near (%.1f, %.1f). Consider adding a node nearby.\", posX, posY),\n237\t\t\t\t\tImpact:   0.25,\n238\t\t\t\t\tPosition: &Point{X: posX, Y: posY, Z: 2.0},\n239\t\t\t\t})\n240\t\t\t}\n241\t\t}\n242\t\n243\t\t// Check for collinear nodes\n244\t\tif nodeCount >= 3 {\n245\t\t\tangles := make([]float64, 0, nodeCount)\n246\t\t\tfor _, node := range nodes.All() {\n247\t\t\t\t// Compute angle from center\n248\t\t\t\tcenterX := (minX + maxX) / 2\n249\t\t\t\tcenterY := (minY + maxY) / 2\n250\t\t\t\tangle := math.Atan2(node.Position.Y-centerY, node.Position.X-centerX)\n251\t\t\t\tangles = append(angles, angle)\n252\t\t\t}\n253\t\n254\t\t\t// Check if all angles are similar (collinear)\n255\t\t\tangleSpread := 0.0\n256\t\t\tfor i := 1; i < len(angles); i++ {\n257\t\t\t\tdiff := math.Abs(angles[i] - angles[0])\n258\t\t\t\tfor diff > math.Pi {\n259\t\t\t\t\tdiff -= 2 * math.Pi\n260\t\t\t\t}\n261\t\t\t\tfor diff < -math.Pi {\n262\t\t\t\t\tdiff += 2 * math.Pi\n263\t\t\t\t}\n264\t\t\t\tangleSpread += diff\n265\t\t\t}\n266\t\t\tangleSpread /= float64(len(angles) - 1)\n267\t\n268\t\t\tif angleSpread < 0.3 { // Less than ~17 degrees spread\n269\t\t\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n270\t\t\t\t\tPriority: \"medium\",\n271\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  \"Nodes appear to be nearly collinear. Spread them out for better coverage.\",\n272\t\t\t\t\tImpact:   0.2,\n273\t\t\t\t})\n274\t\t\t}\n275\t\t}\n276\t\n277\t\t// Estimate improvement with additional nodes\n278\t\tif nodeCount >= 2 && nodeCount < 8 {\n279\t\t\t// Estimate improvement from adding one node\n280\t\t\testimatedImprovement := 0.1 * float64(8-nodeCount) / 6.0\n281\t\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n282\t\t\t\tPriority: \"low\",\n283\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Adding a node could improve accuracy by ~%.0f%%.\", estimatedImprovement*100),\n284\t\t\t\tImpact:   estimatedImprovement,\n285\t\t\t})\n286\t\t}\n287\t\n288\t\t// If no issues found\n289\t\tif len(recs) == 0 {\n290\t\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n291\t\t\t\tPriority: \"low\",\n292\t\t\t\tMessage:  \"Coverage looks good! No specific recommendations.\",\n293\t\t\t\tImpact:   0,\n294\t\t\t})\n295\t\t}\n296\t\n297\t\treturn recs\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t// ShoppingList contains hardware recommendations.\n301\ttype ShoppingList struct {\n302\t\tMinimumNodes      int       `json:\"minimum_nodes\"`\n303\t\tRecommendedNodes  int       `json:\"recommended_nodes\"`\n304\t\tExpectedAccuracy   float64   `json:\"expected_accuracy_m\"`\n305\t\tCoveragePercent   float64   `json:\"coverage_percent\"`\n306\t\tHardwareList      []string  `json:\"hardware_list\"`\n307\t\tAmazonSearchURL   string    `json:\"amazon_search_url\"`\n308\t\tOptimalPositions  []Point   `json:\"optimal_positions,omitempty\"`\n309\t\tCoverageGaps     []Point   `json:\"coverage_gaps,omitempty\"`      // Positions with poor coverage\n310\t\tRecommendedAdditions []NodeAddition `json:\"recommended_additions,omitempty\"` // Specific nodes to add\n311\t\tEstimatedCost     float64   `json:\"estimated_cost_usd,omitempty\"` // Estimated hardware cost in USD\n312\t\tSpaceDimensions   SpaceDimensions `json:\"space_dimensions\"` // Space dimensions for reference\n313\t}\n314\t\n315\t// SpaceDimensions describes the space dimensions\n316\ttype SpaceDimensions struct {\n317\t\tWidth  float64 `json:\"width_m\"`\n318\t\tDepth  float64 `json:\"depth_m\"`\n319\t\tHeight float64 `json:\"height_m\"`\n320\t\tArea   float64 `json:\"area_m2\"`\n321\t\tVolume float64 `json:\"volume_m3\"`\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// NodeAddition represents a specific node to add with position and role\n325\ttype NodeAddition struct {\n326\t\tID          string  `json:\"id\"`\n327\t\tName        string  `json:\"name\"`\n328\t\tPosition    Point   `json:\"position\"`\n329\t\tRole        string  `json:\"role\"`\n330\t\tHeight      string  `json:\"height_description\"` // e.g., \"ceiling\", \"wall\", \"desk\"\n331\t\tImprovement float64 `json:\"estimated_improvement\"` // 0-1, estimated coverage improvement\n332\t}\n333\t\n334\t// GenerateShoppingListFromResults creates a shopping list from simulation results.\n335\tfunc GenerateShoppingListFromResults(space *Space, nodes *NodeSet, coverageScore float64, accuracy AccuracyReport) ShoppingList {\n336\t\tnodeCount := nodes.Count()\n337\t\n338\t\t// Space dimensions\n339\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n340\t\twidth := maxX - minX\n341\t\tdepth := maxY - minY\n342\t\theight := maxZ - minY\n343\t\tarea := width * depth\n344\t\tvolume := width * depth * height\n345\t\n346\t\t// Minimum nodes based on space dimensions\n347\t\tminNodes := int(math.Ceil(area / 30.0)) // ~30 m² per node for fair coverage\n348\t\n349\t\t// Recommended nodes based on desired accuracy\n350\t\trecNodes := minNodes\n351\t\tif accuracy.MedianError > 1.0 && minNodes < 6 {\n352\t\t\trecNodes = minNodes + 1\n353\t\t}\n354\t\tif accuracy.MedianError > 0.8 && minNodes < 8 {\n355\t\t\trecNodes = minNodes + 2\n356\t\t}\n357\t\n358\t\t// Expected accuracy\n359\t\texpectedAccuracy := accuracy.MedianError\n360\t\tif math.IsInf(expectedAccuracy, 0) {\n361\t\t\t// Estimate from node count\n362\t\t\tif nodeCount >= 6 {\n363\t\t\t\texpectedAccuracy = 0.5\n364\t\t\t} else if nodeCount >= 4 {\n365\t\t\t\texpectedAccuracy = 1.0\n366\t\t\t} else {\n367\t\t\t\texpectedAccuracy = 1.5\n368\t\t\t}\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\t// Generate optimal positions (corner + mixed heights)\n372\t\toptimalPositions := generateOptimalPositions(space, recNodes)\n373\t\n374\t\t// Find coverage gaps using GDOP analysis\n375\t\tcoverageGaps := findCoverageGaps(space, nodes)\n376\t\n377\t\t// Generate recommended additions\n378\t\trecommendedAdditions := generateNodeAdditions(space, nodes, coverageGaps)\n379\t\n380\t\t// Hardware list with fmt.Sprintf(\"%d × USB-C Power Supply (5V 2A)\", recNodes))\n384\t\thardware = append(hardware, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d × USB-C Cable (1-2m)\", recNodes))\n385\t\thardware = append(hardware, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d × Adhesive Cable Clips (for mounting)\", recNodes*4))\n386\t\thardware = append(hardware, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d × 3D Printed Case (optional)\", recNodes))\n387\t\n388\t\t// Estimated cost (as of cable\n392\t\t\tfloat64(recNodes)*2.0               // Cable clips\n393\t\n394\t\t// Amazon search URL (non-affiliate)\n395\t\tsearchURL := fmt.Sprintf(\"https://www.amazon.com/s?k=esp32-s3+devkit+usb-c+psram\")\n396\t\n397\t\treturn ShoppingList{\n398\t\t\tMinimumNodes:       minNodes,\n399\t\t\tRecommendedNodes:   recNodes,\n400\t\t\tExpectedAccuracy:   expectedAccuracy,\n401\t\t\tCoveragePercent:    coverageScore,\n402\t\t\tHardwareList:       hardware,\n403\t\t\tAmazonSearchURL:    searchURL,\n404\t\t\tOptimalPositions:   optimalPositions,\n405\t\t\tCoverageGaps:       coverageGaps,\n406\t\t\tRecommendedAdditions: recommendedAdditions,\n407\t\t\tEstimatedCost: volume,\n414\t\t\t},\n415\t\t}\n416\t}\n417\t\n418\t// generateOptimalPositions generates optimal node positions for a given count\n419\tfunc generateOptimalPositions(space *Space, count int) []Point {\n420\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n421\t\tpositions := make([]Point, 0, count)\n422\t\n423\t\t// Strategy: place nodes at corners and mid-points, with mixed heights\n424\t\tcorners := []Point{\n425\t\t\t{X: minX + 0.5, Y: minY + 0.5, Z: 2.2},  // Low corner, high\n426\t\t\t{X: maxX - 0.5, Y: minY + 0.5, Z: 2.2},  // Low corner, high\n427\t\t\t{X: minX + 0.5, Y: maxY - 0.5, Z: 2.2},  // Low corner, high\n428\t\t\t{X: maxX - 0.5, Y: maxY - 0.5, Z: 2.2},  // Low corner, high\n429\t\t\t{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: minY + 0.5, Z: 2.5}, // Mid wall, high\n430\t\t\t{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: maxY - 0.5, Z: 2.5}, // Mid wall, high\n431\t\t\t{X: minX + 0.5, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: 0.3},  // Mid wall, low\n432\t\t\t{X: maxX - 0.5, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: 0.3},  // Mid wall, low\n433\t\t}\n434\t\n435\t\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n436\t\t\tif i < len(corners) {\n437\t\t\t\tpositions = append(positions, corners[i])\n438\t\t\t} else {\n439\t\t\t\t// Add random position for extra nodes\n440\t\t\t\tpositions = append(positions, Point{\n441\t\t\t\t\tX: minX + mrand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n442\t\t\t\t\tY: minY + mrand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n443\t\t\t\t\tZ: 0.3 + mrand.Float64()*2.0, // Mixed height\n444\t\t\t\t})\n445\t\t\t}\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn positions\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// findCoverageGaps finds positions with poor GDOP (coverage gaps)\n452\tfunc findCoverageGaps(space *Space, nodes *NodeSet) []Point {\n453\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n454\t\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n455\t\n456\t\tif len(links) < 2 {\n457\t\t\t// No links means no coverage - return center of space as gap\n458\t\t\treturn []Point{{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: 1.0}}\n459\t\t}\n460\t\n461\t\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n462\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n463\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n464\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n465\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n466\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n467\t\t})\n468\t\n469\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n470\t\tgaps := make([]Point, 0)\n471\t\n472\t\t// Find cells with poor or no coverage\n473\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n474\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n475\t\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"poor\" || cell.Quality == \"none\" {\n476\t\t\t\t\tgaps = append(gaps, Point{X: cell.X, Y: cell.Y, Z: cell.Z})\n477\t\t\t\t}\n478\t\t\t}\n479\t\t}\n480\t\n481\t\t// Limit to top 10 worst coverage gaps\n482\t\tif len(gaps) > 10 {\n483\t\t\tgaps = gaps[:10]\n484\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\treturn gaps\n487\t}\n488\t\n489\t// generateNodeAdditions creates specific node addition recommendations\n490\tfunc generateNodeAdditions(space *Space, nodes *NodeSet, gaps []Point) []NodeAddition {\n491\t\tadditions := make([]NodeAddition, 0)\n492\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n493\t\n494\t\t// If we have coverage gaps, suggest adding nodes there\n495\t\tfor i, gap := range gaps {\n496\t\t\tif i >= 3 {\n497\t\t\t\tbreak // Limit to 3 gap-based additions\n498\t\t\t}\n499\t\n500\t\t\theightDesc := \"ceiling\"\n501\t\t\tif gap.Z < 1.0 {\n502\t\t\t\theightDesc = \"wall mount\"\n503\t\t\t} else if gap.Z > 2.0 {\n504\t\t\t\theightDesc = \"ceiling\"\n505\t\t\t} else {\n506\t\t\t\theightDesc = \"high wall\"\n507\t\t\t}\n508\t\n509\t\t\tadditions = append(additions, NodeAddition{\n510\t\t\t\tID:          fmt.Sprintf(\"node-gap-%d\", i+1),\n511\t\t\t\tName:        fmt.Sprintf(\"Gap Coverage Node %d\", i+1),\n512\t\t\t\tPosition:    gap,\n513\t\t\t\tRole:        \"tx_rx\",\n514\t\t\t\tHeight:      heightDesc,\n515\t\t\t\tImprovement: 0.2 + float64(3-i)*0.05, // Later gaps have lower priority\n516\t\t\t})\n517\t\t}\n518\t\n519\t\t// Suggest corner nodes if we have few nodes\n520\t\tif nodes.Count() < 4 {\n521\t\t\tcorners := CornerPositions(space)\n522\t\t\tfor i := nodes.Count(); i < 4 && i < len(corners); i++ {\n523\t\t\t\theightDesc := \"ceiling\"\n524\t\t\t\tif corners[i].Z < 1.0 {\n525\t\t\t\t\theightDesc = \"low\"\n526\t\t\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t\t\tadditions = append(additions, NodeAddition{\n529\t\t\t\t\tID:          fmt.Sprintf(\"node-corner-%d\", i+1),\n530\t\t\t\t\tName:        fmt.Sprintf(\"Corner Node %d\", i+1),\n531\t\t\t\t\tPosition:    corners[i],\n532\t\t\t\t\tRole:        \"tx_rx\",\n533\t\t\t\t\tHeight:      heightDesc,\n534\t\t\t\t\tImprovement: 0.15,\n535\t\t\t\t})\n536\t\t\t}\n537\t\t}\n538\t\n539\t\t// If no specific additions, suggest a center node\n540\t\tif len(additions) == 0 {\n541\t\t\tadditions = append(additions, NodeAddition{\n542\t\t\t\tID:          \"node-center-1\",\n543\t\t\t\tName:        \"Center Node\",\n544\t\t\t\tPosition:    Point{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: 2.0},\n545\t\t\t\tRole:        \"tx_rx\",\n546\t\t\t\tHeight:      \"ceiling\",\n547\t\t\t\tImprovement: 0.1,\n548\t\t\t})\n549\t\t}\n550\t\n551\t\treturn additions\n552\t}\n553\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b3b417b-ffca-4e51-853f-9cbdc68e2df1","uuid":"b53aebe7-cdbe-40ad-a576-1b9fbf71b5d1","timestamp":"2026-05-06T03:27:49.115Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/simulator/accuracy.go","content":"// Package simulator provides accuracy estimation for the pre-deployment simulator.\npackage simulator\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\tmrand \"math/rand\"\n)\n\n// AccuracyEstimator computes accuracy metrics from simulation results.\ntype AccuracyEstimator struct{}\n\n// NewAccuracyEstimator creates a new accuracy estimator.\nfunc NewAccuracyEstimator() *AccuracyEstimator {\n\treturn &AccuracyEstimator{}\n}\n\n// AccuracyReport contains accuracy metrics from a simulation run.\ntype AccuracyReport struct {\n\tMedianError          float64 `json:\"median_error_m\"`          // Median position error in meters\n\tMeanError            float64 `json:\"mean_error_m\"`            // Mean position error in meters\n\tMaxError             float64 `json:\"max_error_m\"`             // Maximum position error in meters\n\tP95Error             float64 `json:\"p95_error_m\"`             // 95th percentile error\n\tDetectionRate        float64 `json:\"detection_rate\"`           // Fraction of walkers detected\n\tFalsePositiveRate    float64 `json:\"false_positive_rate\"`     // False positives per second\n\tRecallAt1m          float64 `json:\"recall_at_1m\"`            // Fraction within 1m of true position\n\tRecallAt2m          float64 `json:\"recall_at_2m\"`            // Fraction within 2m of true position\n\tSampleCount          int     `json:\"sample_count\"`            // Number of walker positions evaluated\n}\n\n// Recommendation is a deployment recommendation.\ntype Recommendation struct {\n\tPriority   string  `json:\"priority\"`   // \"high\", \"medium\", \"low\"\n\tMessage    string  `json:\"message\"`    // Human-readable recommendation\n\tImpact     float64 `json:\"impact\"`     // Estimated improvement (0-1)\n\tPosition   *Point  `json:\"position,omitempty\"` // Suggested position (if applicable)\n}\n\n// RecommendationEngine generates deployment recommendations.\ntype RecommendationEngine struct{}\n\n// NewRecommendationEngine creates a new recommendation engine.\nfunc NewRecommendationEngine() *RecommendationEngine {\n\treturn &RecommendationEngine{}\n}\n\n// Compute evaluates accuracy metrics from walker positions and blob detections.\nfunc (ae *AccuracyEstimator) Compute(walkers []*SimWalker, blobs []BlobResult) AccuracyReport {\n\tif len(walkers) == 0 {\n\t\treturn AccuracyReport{}\n\t}\n\n\t// Collect all true positions and matched blob positions\n\ttruePositions := make([]Point, 0)\n\tdetectedPositions := make([]Point, 0)\n\terrors := make([]float64, 0)\n\n\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n\t\tfor _, truePos := range walker.TrueHistory {\n\t\t\ttruePositions = append(truePositions, truePos)\n\n\t\t\t// Find nearest blob\n\t\t\tnearestDist := math.Inf(1)\n\t\t\tfor _, blob := range blobs {\n\t\t\t\tif blob.WalkerID == walker.ID {\n\t\t\t\t\tdist := blob.Position.Distance(truePos)\n\t\t\t\t\tif dist < nearestDist {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tnearestDist = dist\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(nearestDist, 1) {\n\t\t\t\tdetectedPositions = append(detectedPositions, truePos)\n\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, nearestDist)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn AccuracyReport{\n\t\t\tMedianError:    math.Inf(1),\n\t\t\tMeanError:      math.Inf(1),\n\t\t\tMaxError:       math.Inf(1),\n\t\t\tDetectionRate:  0,\n\t\t\tSampleCount:    len(truePositions),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute statistics\n\tmeanError := 0.0\n\tfor _, e := range errors {\n\t\tmeanError += e\n\t}\n\tmeanError /= float64(len(errors))\n\n\t// Median error\n\tsortedErrors := make([]float64, len(errors))\n\tcopy(sortedErrors, errors)\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(sortedErrors); i++ {\n\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(sortedErrors); j++ {\n\t\t\tif sortedErrors[i] > sortedErrors[j] {\n\t\t\t\tsortedErrors[i], sortedErrors[j] = sortedErrors[j], sortedErrors[i]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tmedianError := sortedErrors[len(sortedErrors)/2]\n\n\t// Max error\n\tmaxError := sortedErrors[len(sortedErrors)-1]\n\n\t// 95th percentile\n\tp95Index := int(float64(len(sortedErrors)) * 0.95)\n\tif p95Index >= len(sortedErrors) {\n\t\tp95Index = len(sortedErrors) - 1\n\t}\n\tp95Error := sortedErrors[p95Index]\n\n\t// Detection rate\n\tdetectionRate := float64(len(detectedPositions)) / float64(len(truePositions))\n\n\t// Recall at 1m and 2m\n\trecall1m := 0.0\n\trecall2m := 0.0\n\tfor _, e := range errors {\n\t\tif e <= 1.0 {\n\t\t\trecall1m++\n\t\t}\n\t\tif e <= 2.0 {\n\t\t\trecall2m++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\trecall1m /= float64(len(errors))\n\trecall2m /= float64(len(errors))\n\n\t// False positive rate (blobs without matching walker)\n\tfalsePositives := 0\n\tfor _, blob := range blobs {\n\t\thasMatch := false\n\t\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n\t\t\tif blob.WalkerID == walker.ID {\n\t\t\t\thasMatch = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !hasMatch {\n\t\t\tfalsePositives++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfalsePositiveRate := float64(falsePositives) / float64(len(errors))\n\n\treturn AccuracyReport{\n\t\tMedianError:       medianError,\n\t\tMeanError:         meanError,\n\t\tMaxError:          maxError,\n\t\tP95Error:          p95Error,\n\t\tDetectionRate:     detectionRate,\n\t\tFalsePositiveRate: falsePositiveRate,\n\t\tRecallAt1m:        recall1m,\n\t\tRecallAt2m:        recall2m,\n\t\tSampleCount:       len(errors),\n\t}\n}\n\n// Generate generates recommendations based on space, nodes, GDOP, and coverage.\nfunc (re *RecommendationEngine) Generate(space *Space, nodes *NodeSet, gdopMap []float64, coverageScore float64) []Recommendation {\n\trecs := make([]Recommendation, 0)\n\n\t// Check coverage score\n\tif coverageScore < 50 {\n\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n\t\t\tPriority: \"high\",\n\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Coverage is below 50%% (%.0f%%). Consider adding more nodes.\", coverageScore),\n\t\t\tImpact:   0.3,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Check node count\n\tnodeCount := nodes.Count()\n\tif nodeCount < 4 {\n\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n\t\t\tPriority: \"medium\",\n\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Only %d nodes. For best accuracy, use at least 4 nodes.\", nodeCount),\n\t\t\tImpact:   0.2,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Check height diversity\n\thasLow, hasHigh := false, false\n\tfor _, node := range nodes.All() {\n\t\tif node.Position.Z < 1.0 {\n\t\t\thasLow = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tif node.Position.Z > 2.0 {\n\t\t\thasHigh = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif !hasLow || !hasHigh {\n\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n\t\t\tPriority: \"medium\",\n\t\t\tMessage:  \"For better Z-axis accuracy, place nodes at mixed heights (some low, some high).\",\n\t\t\tImpact:   0.15,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Find worst coverage areas\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\tif len(gdopMap) > 0 {\n\t\t// Find cells with worst GDOP (highest values, excluding infinity)\n\t\tmaxGDOP := 0.0\n\t\tworstIdx := -1\n\n\t\tfor i, gdop := range gdopMap {\n\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(gdop, 0) && gdop > maxGDOP {\n\t\t\t\tmaxGDOP = gdop\n\t\t\t\tworstIdx = i\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif maxGDOP > 8.0 && worstIdx >= 0 {\n\t\t\t// Compute position from index\n\t\t\twidthCells := int(math.Ceil((maxX - minX) / 0.2))\n\t\t\tdepthCells := int(math.Ceil((maxY - minY) / 0.2))\n\n\t\t\t_ = worstIdx / (widthCells * depthCells) // z-layer index, not used in 2D recommendation\n\t\t\tremainder := worstIdx % (widthCells * depthCells)\n\t\t\tx := remainder / depthCells\n\t\t\ty := remainder % depthCells\n\n\t\t\tposX := minX + float64(x)*0.2 + 0.1\n\t\t\tposY := minY + float64(y)*0.2 + 0.1\n\n\t\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n\t\t\t\tPriority: \"high\",\n\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Poor coverage detected near (%.1f, %.1f). Consider adding a node nearby.\", posX, posY),\n\t\t\t\tImpact:   0.25,\n\t\t\t\tPosition: &Point{X: posX, Y: posY, Z: 2.0},\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for collinear nodes\n\tif nodeCount >= 3 {\n\t\tangles := make([]float64, 0, nodeCount)\n\t\tfor _, node := range nodes.All() {\n\t\t\t// Compute angle from center\n\t\t\tcenterX := (minX + maxX) / 2\n\t\t\tcenterY := (minY + maxY) / 2\n\t\t\tangle := math.Atan2(node.Position.Y-centerY, node.Position.X-centerX)\n\t\t\tangles = append(angles, angle)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if all angles are similar (collinear)\n\t\tangleSpread := 0.0\n\t\tfor i := 1; i < len(angles); i++ {\n\t\t\tdiff := math.Abs(angles[i] - angles[0])\n\t\t\tfor diff > math.Pi {\n\t\t\t\tdiff -= 2 * math.Pi\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor diff < -math.Pi {\n\t\t\t\tdiff += 2 * math.Pi\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tangleSpread += diff\n\t\t}\n\t\tangleSpread /= float64(len(angles) - 1)\n\n\t\tif angleSpread < 0.3 { // Less than ~17 degrees spread\n\t\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n\t\t\t\tPriority: \"medium\",\n\t\t\t\tMessage:  \"Nodes appear to be nearly collinear. Spread them out for better coverage.\",\n\t\t\t\tImpact:   0.2,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Estimate improvement with additional nodes\n\tif nodeCount >= 2 && nodeCount < 8 {\n\t\t// Estimate improvement from adding one node\n\t\testimatedImprovement := 0.1 * float64(8-nodeCount) / 6.0\n\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n\t\t\tPriority: \"low\",\n\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Adding a node could improve accuracy by ~%.0f%%.\", estimatedImprovement*100),\n\t\t\tImpact:   estimatedImprovement,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// If no issues found\n\tif len(recs) == 0 {\n\t\trecs = append(recs, Recommendation{\n\t\t\tPriority: \"low\",\n\t\t\tMessage:  \"Coverage looks good! No specific recommendations.\",\n\t\t\tImpact:   0,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn recs\n}\n\n// ShoppingList contains hardware recommendations.\ntype ShoppingList struct {\n\tMinimumNodes      int       `json:\"minimum_nodes\"`\n\tRecommendedNodes  int       `json:\"recommended_nodes\"`\n\tExpectedAccuracy   float64   `json:\"expected_accuracy_m\"`\n\tCoveragePercent   float64   `json:\"coverage_percent\"`\n\tHardwareList      []string  `json:\"hardware_list\"`\n\tAmazonSearchURL   string    `json:\"amazon_search_url\"`\n\tOptimalPositions  []Point   `json:\"optimal_positions,omitempty\"`\n\tCoverageGaps     []Point   `json:\"coverage_gaps,omitempty\"`      // Positions with poor coverage\n\tRecommendedAdditions []NodeAddition `json:\"recommended_additions,omitempty\"` // Specific nodes to add\n\tEstimatedCost     float64   `json:\"estimated_cost_usd,omitempty\"` // Estimated hardware cost in USD\n\tSpaceDimensions   SpaceDimensions `json:\"space_dimensions\"` // Space dimensions for reference\n}\n\n// SpaceDimensions describes the space dimensions\ntype SpaceDimensions struct {\n\tWidth  float64 `json:\"width_m\"`\n\tDepth  float64 `json:\"depth_m\"`\n\tHeight float64 `json:\"height_m\"`\n\tArea   float64 `json:\"area_m2\"`\n\tVolume float64 `json:\"volume_m3\"`\n}\n\n// NodeAddition represents a specific node to add with position and role\ntype NodeAddition struct {\n\tID          string  `json:\"id\"`\n\tName        string  `json:\"name\"`\n\tPosition    Point   `json:\"position\"`\n\tRole        string  `json:\"role\"`\n\tHeight      string  `json:\"height_description\"` // e.g., \"ceiling\", \"wall\", \"desk\"\n\tImprovement float64 `json:\"estimated_improvement\"` // 0-1, estimated coverage improvement\n}\n\n// GenerateShoppingListFromResults creates a shopping list from simulation results.\nfunc GenerateShoppingListFromResults(space *Space, nodes *NodeSet, coverageScore float64, accuracy AccuracyReport) ShoppingList {\n\tnodeCount := nodes.Count()\n\n\t// Space dimensions\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n\twidth := maxX - minX\n\tdepth := maxY - minY\n\theight := maxZ - minY\n\tarea := width * depth\n\tvolume := width * depth * height\n\n\t// Minimum nodes based on space dimensions\n\tminNodes := int(math.Ceil(area / 30.0)) // ~30 m² per node for fair coverage\n\n\t// Recommended nodes based on desired accuracy\n\trecNodes := minNodes\n\tif accuracy.MedianError > 1.0 && minNodes < 6 {\n\t\trecNodes = minNodes + 1\n\t}\n\tif accuracy.MedianError > 0.8 && minNodes < 8 {\n\t\trecNodes = minNodes + 2\n\t}\n\n\t// Expected accuracy\n\texpectedAccuracy := accuracy.MedianError\n\tif math.IsInf(expectedAccuracy, 0) {\n\t\t// Estimate from node count\n\t\tif nodeCount >= 6 {\n\t\t\texpectedAccuracy = 0.5\n\t\t} else if nodeCount >= 4 {\n\t\t\texpectedAccuracy = 1.0\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\texpectedAccuracy = 1.5\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate optimal positions (corner + mixed heights)\n\toptimalPositions := generateOptimalPositions(space, recNodes)\n\n\t// Find coverage gaps using GDOP analysis\n\tcoverageGaps := findCoverageGaps(space, nodes)\n\n\t// Generate recommended fmt.Sprintf(\"%d × USB-C Cable (1-2m)\", recNodes))\n\thardware = append(hardware, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d × Adhesive Cable Clips (for mounting)\", recNodes*4))\n\thardware = append(hardware, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d × 3D Printed Case (optional)\", recNodes))\n\n\t// Estimated cost (as of 2025)\n\testimatedCost := float64(recNodes)*15.0 + // ESP32-S3 dev board\n\t\tfloat64(recNodes)*8.0 +              // Power supply\n\t\tfloat64(recNodes)*3.0 +              // USB cable\n\t\tfloat64(recNodes)*2.0               // Cable clips\n\n\t// Amazon search URL (non-affiliate)\n\tsearchURL := fmt.Sprintf(\"https://www.amazon.com/s?k=esp32-s3+devkit+usb-c+psram\")\n\n\treturn ShoppingList{\n\t\tMinimumNodes:       minNodes,\n\t\tRecommendedNodes:   recNodes,\n\t\tExpectedAccuracy:   expectedAccuracy,\n\t\tCoveragePercent:    coverageScore,\n\t\tHardwareList:       hardware,\n\t\tAmazonSearchURL:    searchURL,\n\t\tOptimalPositions:   optimalPositions,\n\t\tCoverageGaps:       coverageGaps,\n\t\tRecommendedAdditions: recommendedAdditions,\n\t\tEstimatedCost:      estimatedCost,\n\t\tSpaceDimensions: SpaceDimensions{\n\t\t\tWidth:  width,\n\t\t\tDepth:  depth,\n\t\t\tHeight: height,\n\t\t\tArea:   area,\n\t\t\tVolume: volume,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// generateOptimalPositions generates optimal node positions for a given count\nfunc generateOptimalPositions(space *Space, count int) []Point {\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\tpositions := make([]Point, 0, count)\n\n\t// Strategy: place nodes at corners and mid-points, with mixed heights\n\tcorners := []Point{\n\t\t{X: minX + 0.5, Y: minY + 0.5, Z: 2.2},  // Low corner, high\n\t\t{X: maxX - 0.5, Y: minY + 0.5, Z: 2.2},  // Low corner, high\n\t\t{X: minX + 0.5, Y: maxY - 0.5, Z: 2.2},  // Low corner, high\n\t\t{X: maxX - 0.5, Y: maxY - 0.5, Z: 2.2},  // Low corner, high\n\t\t{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: minY + 0.5, Z: 2.5}, // Mid wall, high\n\t\t{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: maxY - 0.5, Z: 2.5}, // Mid wall, high\n\t\t{X: minX + 0.5, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: 0.3},  // Mid wall, low\n\t\t{X: maxX - 0.5, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: 0.3},  // Mid wall, low\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\tif i < len(corners) {\n\t\t\tpositions = append(positions, corners[i])\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Add random position for extra nodes\n\t\t\tpositions = append(positions, Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: minX + mrand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n\t\t\t\tY: minY + mrand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n\t\t\t\tZ: 0.3 + mrand.Float64()*2.0, // Mixed height\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn positions\n}\n\n// findCoverageGaps finds positions with poor GDOP (coverage gaps)\nfunc findCoverageGaps(space *Space, nodes *NodeSet) []Point {\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\n\tif len(links) < 2 {\n\t\t// No links means no coverage - return center of space as gap\n\t\treturn []Point{{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: 1.0}}\n\t}\n\n\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t})\n\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\tgaps := make([]Point, 0)\n\n\t// Find cells with poor or no coverage\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"poor\" || cell.Quality == \"none\" {\n\t\t\t\tgaps = append(gaps, Point{X: cell.X, Y: cell.Y, Z: cell.Z})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Limit to top 10 worst coverage gaps\n\tif len(gaps) > 10 {\n\t\tgaps = gaps[:10]\n\t}\n\n\treturn gaps\n}\n\n// generateNodeAdditions creates specific node addition recommendations\nfunc generateNodeAdditions(space *Space, nodes *NodeSet, gaps []Point) []NodeAddition {\n\tadditions := make([]NodeAddition, 0)\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\n\t// If we have coverage gaps, suggest adding nodes there\n\tfor i, gap := range gaps {\n\t\tif i >= 3 {\n\t\t\tbreak // Limit to 3 gap-based additions\n\t\t}\n\n\t\theightDesc := \"ceiling\"\n\t\tif gap.Z < 1.0 {\n\t\t\theightDesc = \"wall mount\"\n\t\t} else if gap.Z > 2.0 {\n\t\t\theightDesc = \"ceiling\"\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\theightDesc = \"high wall\"\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tadditions = append(additions, NodeAddition{\n\t\t\tID:          fmt.Sprintf(\"node-gap-%d\", i+1),\n\t\t\tName:        fmt.Sprintf(\"Gap Coverage Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\tPosition:    gap,\n\t\t\tRole:        \"tx_rx\",\n\t\t\tHeight:      heightDesc,\n\t\t\tImprovement: 0.2 + float64(3-i)*0.05, // Later gaps have lower priority\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Suggest corner nodes if we have few nodes\n\tif nodes.Count() < 4 {\n\t\tcorners := CornerPositions(space)\n\t\tfor i := nodes.Count(); i < 4 && i < len(corners); i++ {\n\t\t\theightDesc := \"ceiling\"\n\t\t\tif corners[i].Z < 1.0 {\n\t\t\t\theightDesc = \"low\"\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tadditions = append(additions, NodeAddition{\n\t\t\t\tID:          fmt.Sprintf(\"node-corner-%d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tName:        fmt.Sprintf(\"Corner Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tPosition:    corners[i],\n\t\t\t\tRole:        \"tx_rx\",\n\t\t\t\tHeight:      heightDesc,\n\t\t\t\tImprovement: 0.15,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If no specific additions, suggest a center node\n\tif len(additions) == 0 {\n\t\tadditions = append(additions, NodeAddition{\n\t\t\tID:          \"node-center-1\",\n\t\t\tName:        \"Center Node\",\n\t\t\tPosition:    Point{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: 2.0},\n\t\t\tRole:        \"tx_rx\",\n\t\t\tHeight:      \"ceiling\",\n\t\t\tImprovement: 0.1,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn additions\n}\n","numLines":553,"startLine":1,"totalLines":553}}}

the 2D line segment\n111\t// from a to b (projected on the XY plane)\n112\tfunc (w *WallSegment) IntersectsLine(a, b Point) bool {\n113\t\t// Project to 2D (ignore Z for wall intersection check)\n114\t\tax, ay := a.X, a.Y\n115\t\tbx, by := b.X, b.Y\n116\t\twx1, wy1 := w.P1.X, w.P1.Y\n117\t\twx2, wy2 := w.P2.X, w.P2.Y\n118\t\n119\t\t// Compute orientation\n120\t\torientation := func(p1x, p1y, p2x, p2y, px, py float64) float64 {\n121\t\t\treturn (p2x-p1x)*(py-p1y) - (p2y-p1y)*(px-p1x)\n122\t\t}\n123\t\n124\t\t// Check if line segments intersect\n125\t\to1 := orientation(ax, // m/s\n)\n\n// WallMaterial represents different wall types and their RF attenuation\ntype WallMaterial string\n\nconst (\n\tMaterialDrywall  WallMaterial = \"drywall\"\n\tMaterialBrick    WallMaterial = \"brick\"\n\tMaterialConcrete WallMaterial = \"concrete\"\n\tMaterialGlass    WallMaterial = \"glass\"\n\tMaterialMetal    WallMaterial = \"metal\"\n)\n\n// WallPenetrationLoss returns dB loss for each material type\nfunc WallPenetrationLoss(m WallMaterial) float64 {\n\tswitch m {\n\tcase MaterialDrywall:\n\t\treturn 3.0\n\tcase MaterialBrick, MaterialConcrete:\n\t\treturn 10.0\n\tcase MaterialGlass:\n\t\treturn 2.0\n\tcase MaterialMetal:\n\t\treturn 20.0\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn 3.0 // Default to drywall\n\t}\n}\n\n// Point represents a 3D point in meters\ntype Point struct{ X, Y, Z float64 }\n\n// NewPoint creates a new 3D point\nfunc NewPoint(x, y, z float64) Point {\n\treturn Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: z}\n}\n\n// Distance returns Euclidean distance to another point\nfunc (p Point) Distance(to Point) float64 {\n\tdx := to.X - p.X\n\tdy := to.Y - p.Y\n\tdz := to.Z - p.Z\n\treturn math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)\n}\n\n// Vector returns the direction vector from p to to (normalized)\nfunc (p Point) Vector(to Point) Point {\n\td := p.Distance(to)\n\tif d < 1e-9 {\n\t\treturn Point{0, 0, 0}\n\t}\n\treturn Point{\n\t\tX: (to.X - p.X) / d,\n\t\tY: (to.Y - := a.X, a.Y\n\tbx, by := b.X, b.Y\n\twx1, wy1 := w.P1.X, w.P1.Y\n\twx2, wy2 := w.P2.X, w.P2.Y\n\n\t// Compute orientation\n\torientation := func(p1x, p1y, p2x, p2y, px, py float64) float64
