============================================\n    // 3D Visualization\n    // ============================================\n    function updateNodeVisualization(node) {\n        // Get scene from Viz3D\n        const scene = window.Viz3D?.getScene?.();\n        if (!scene) return;\n\n        // Remove existing mesh\n        removeNodeVisualization(node.id);\n\n        let mesh;\n\n        if (node.virtual) {\n            // Ghost wireframe node for virtual nodes\n            // Use wireframe octahedron (matching viz3d.js style)\n            const geometry = new THREE.OctahedronGeometry(0.15, 0);\n            const material = new THREE.MeshPhongMaterial({\n                color: 0x80cbc4,  // Teal for virtual nodes\n                wireframe: true,\n                transparent: true,\n                opacity: 0.6,\n            });\n\n            mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geometry, material);\n            mesh.userData = {\n                nodeId: node.id,\n                mac: node.mac,\n                virtual: true,\n                node_type: node.node_type || 'esp32',\n            };\n        } else {\n            // Solid sphere for real nodes\n            const geometry = new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.15, 16, 16);\n            const material = new THREE.MeshLambertMaterial({\n                color: node.role === 'tx' ? 0xff6b6b : node.role === 'rx' ? 0x4ecdc4 : 0x45b7d1,\n                emissive: node.role === 'tx' ? 0xff6b6b : node.role === 'rx' ? 0x4ecdc4 : 0x45b7d1,\n                emissiveIntensity: 0.3,\n            });\n\n            mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geometry, material);\n            mesh.userData = {\n                nodeId: node.id,\n                mac: node.mac || '',\n                virtual: false,\n            };\n        }\n\n        mesh.position.set(node.position.x, node.position.y, node.position.z);\n\n        scene.add(mesh);\n        _nodeMeshes.set(node.id, mesh);\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Link Lines (with dashed lines for virtual nodes)\n    // ============================================\n    function rebuildLinkLines() {\n        const scene = window.Viz3D?.getScene?.();\n        if (!scene) return;\n\n        // Clear existing link lines\n        clearLinkLines();\n\n        if (state.nodes.length < 2) return;\n\n        // Create links between all node pairs\n        for (let i = 0; i < state.nodes.length; i++) {\n            for (let j = i + 1; j < state.nodes.length; j++) {\n                const nodeA = state.nodes[i];\n                const nodeB = state.nodes[j];\n                const meshA = _nodeMeshes.get(nodeA.id);\n                const meshB = _nodeMeshes.get(nodeB.id);\n\n                if (!meshA || !meshB) continue;\n\n                // Check if either node is virtual\n                const aIsVirtual = nodeA.virtual || false;\n                const bIsVirtual = nodeB.virtual || false;\n                const isVirtualLink = aIsVirtual || bIsVirtual;\n\n                // Create line geometry\n                const points = [\n                    meshA.position.clone(),\n                    meshB.position.clone()\n                ];\n                const geometry = new THREE.BufferGeometry().setFromPoints(points);\n\n                let material;\n                if (isVirtualLink) {\n                    // Dashed line for virtual links (teal color)\n                    material = new THREE.LineDashedMaterial({\n                        color: 0x80cbc4,\n                        dashSize: 0.1,\n                        gapSize: 0.05,\n                        opacity: 0.5,\n                        transparent: true,\n                    });\n                } else {\n                    // Solid line for real links\n                    material = new THREE.LineBasicMaterial({\n                        color: 0x4fc3f7,\n                        opacity: 0.3,\n                        transparent: true,\n                    });\n                }\n\n                const line = new THREE.Line(geometry, material);\n                line.userData = {\n                    nodeA: nodeA.id,\n                    nodeB: nodeB.id,\n                    virtual: isVirtualLink\n                };\n\n                if (isVirtualLink) {\n                    line.computeLineDistances(); // Required for dashed lines\n                }\n\n                scene.add(line);\n                _linkLineMeshes.push(line);\n            }\n        }\n\n        console.log('[Simulate] Rebuilt', _linkLineMeshes.length, 'link lines');\n    }\n\n    function clearLinkLines() {\n        const scene = window.Viz3D?.getScene?.();\n        if (!scene) return;\n\n        for (const line of _linkLineMeshes) {\n            scene.remove(line);\n            line.geometry.dispose();\n            line.material.dispose();\n        }\n        _linkLineMeshes = [];\n    }\n\n    function removeNodeVisualization(nodeId) {\n        const mesh = _nodeMeshes.get(nodeId);\n        if (mesh) {\n            const scene = window.Viz3D?.getScene?.();\n            if (scene) scene.remove(mesh);\n            mesh.geometry.dispose();\n            mesh.material.dispose();\n            _nodeMeshes.delete(nodeId);\n        }\n\n        // Rebuild link lines after removing a node\n        rebuildLinkLines();\n    }\n\n    function updateWalkerVisualization(walker) {\n        // Get scene from Viz3D\n        const scene = window.Viz3D?.getScene?.();\n        if (!scene) return;\n\n        // Remove existing mesh\n        removeWalkerVisualization(walker.id);\n\n        // Create walker mesh (capsule for person)\n        const geometry = new THREE.CapsuleGeometry(0.1, 0.5, 4, 8);\n        const material = new THREE.MeshLambertMaterial({\n            color: 0xffa726,\n            transparent: true,\n            opacity: 0.8,\n        });\n\n        const mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geometry, material);\n        mesh.position.set(walker.position.x, walker.position.y, walker.position.z);\n        mesh.userData.walkerId = walker.id;\n\n        scene.add(mesh);\n        _walkerMeshes.set(walker.id, mesh);\n    }\n\n    function removeWalkerVisualization(walkerId) {\n        const mesh = _walkerMeshes.get(walkerId);\n        if (mesh) {\n            const scene = window.Viz3D?.getScene?.();\n            if (scene) scene.remove(mesh);\n            mesh.geometry.dispose();\n            mesh.material.dispose();\n            _walkerMeshes.delete(walkerId);\n        }\n    }\n\n    function clearSimulationMeshes() {\n        _nodeMeshes.forEach((mesh, id) => removeNodeVisualization(id));\n        _walkerMeshes.forEach((mesh, id) => removeWalkerVisualization(id));\n        clearLinkLines();\n        clearGDOPMesh();\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Public API\n    // ============================================\n    window.SpaxelSimulator = {\n        init: init,\n        getState: () => state,\n        addNode: addNode,\n        removeNode: removeNode,\n        applySpace: applySpace,\n        toggleGDOP: toggleGDOP,\n        updateGDOP: updateGDOP,\n    };\n\n    // Auto-initialize when DOM is ready\n    if (document.readyState === 'loading') {\n        document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', init);\n    } else {\n        init();\n    }\n\n    console.log('[Simulate] Simulator module loaded');\n})();\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":690,"oldLines":10,"newStart":690,"newLines":6,"lines":["         state.simulationPaused = false;","         state.simulationTime = 0;"," ","- (\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"math\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5\"\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/simulator\"\n)\n\n// SimulatorHandler manages pre-deployment simulation API endpoints.\n// It allows users to define virtual spaces, place virtual nodes, simulate walkers,\n// and compute GDOP coverage quality before purchasing hardware.\ntype SimulatorHandler struct {\n\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n\tspace    *simulator.Space\n\tnodes    *simulator.NodeSet\n\twalkers  *simulator.WalkerSet\n}\n\n// NewSimulatorHandler creates a new simulator handler.\nfunc NewSimulatorHandler() *SimulatorHandler {\n\t// Start with a default space\n\treturn &SimulatorHandler{\n\t\tspace:   simulator.DefaultSpace(),\n\t\tnodes:   simulator.NewNodeSet(),\n\t\twalkers: simulator.NewWalkerSet(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// RegisterRoutes registers simulator routes on the router.\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n\tr.Route(\"/simulator\", func(r chi.Router) {\n\t\tr.Get(\"/\", h.GetState)\n\t\tr.Post(\"/reset\", h.Reset)\n\t\tr.Post(\"/session\", h.CreateSession)\n\n\t\t// Space management\n\t\tr.Route(\"/space\", func(r chi.Router) {\n\t\t\tr.Get(\"/\", h.GetSpace)\n\t\t\tr.Put(\"/\", h.SetSpace)\n\t\t\tr.Post(\"/validate\", h.ValidateSpace)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Node management\n\t\tr.Route(\"/nodes\", func(r chi.Router) {\n\t\t\tr.Get(\"/\", h.GetNodes)\n\t\t\tr.Post(\"/\", h.AddNode)\n\t\t\tr.Route(\"/{nodeID}\", func(r chi.Router) {\n\t\t\t\tr.Delete(\"/\", h.RemoveNode)\n\t\t\t\tr.Put(\"/\", h.UpdateNode)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tr.Post(\"/suggest\", h.SuggestNodes)\n\t\t\tr.Post(\"/optimize\", h.OptimizeNodes)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Walker management\n\t\tr.Route(\"/walkers\", func(r chi.Router) {\n\t\t\tr.Get(\"/\", h.GetWalkers)\n\t\t\tr.Post(\"/\", h.AddWalker)\n\t\t\tr.Post(\"/random\", h.AddRandomWalkers)\n\t\t\tr.Post(\"/path\", h.AddPathWalkers)\n\t\t\tr.Delete(\"/{walkerID}\", h.RemoveWalker)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// GDOP computation\n\t\tr.Route(\"/gdop\", func(r chi.Router) {\n\t\t\tr.Post(\"/compute\", h.ComputeGDOP)\n\t\t\tr.Get(\"/coverage\", h.GetCoverageScore)\n\t\t\tr.Get(\"/heatmap\", h.GetGDOPHeatmap)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Shopping list\n\t\tr.Get(\"/shopping-list\", h.GetShoppingList)\n\n\t\t// Simulation\n\t\tr.Post(\"/simulate\", h.RunSimulation)\n\t})\n}\n\n// GetState returns the complete simulator state\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tstate := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"space\":   h.space,\n\t\t\"nodes\":   h.nodes.All(),\n\t\t\"walkers\": h.walkers.All(),\n\t}\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, state)\n}\n\n// Reset resets the simulator to default state\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) Reset(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\n\th.space = simulator.DefaultSpace()\n\th.nodes = simulator.NewNodeSet()\n\th.walkers = simulator.NewWalkerSet()\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{\"status\": \"reset\"})\n}\n\n// CreateSession creates a new simulator session\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) CreateSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req struct {\n\t\tSpace *simulator.Space `json:\"space\"`\n\t}\n\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\t// Use default space if request body is empty/invalid\n\t\treq.Space = simulator.DefaultSpace()\n\t}\n\n\tif req.Space == nil {\n\t\treq.Space = simulator.DefaultSpace()\n\t}\n\n\t// Update the handler's space if provided\n\tif req.Space != nil current configuration\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) ComputeGDOP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req GDOPRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\t// Use defaults if request body is empty\n\t\treq = GDOPRequest{\n\t\t\tCellSize:  0.2,\n\t\t\tMaxZone:   3,\n\t\t\tThreshold: 0.02,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"need at least 2 nodes for GDOP computation\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\n\t// Generate links\n\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\n\t// Create GDOP computer\n\tconfig := simulator.GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: req.CellSize,\n\t}\n\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n\tif req.MaxZone > 0 {\n\t\tgdopComp.SetMaxZone(req.MaxZone)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute GDOP\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\n\t// Compute statistics\n\tcoverageScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n\tavgGDOP := space)\n\n\tresponse := GDOPResponse{\n\t\tResults:        results,\n\t\tCoverageScore:  coverageScore,\n\t\tAverageGDOP:    avgGDOP,\n\t\tQualityCounts:  qualityCounts,\n\t\tDeadZones:      deadZones,\n\t\tRecommendedPos: recommendedPos,\n\t\tLinks:          links,\n\t}\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// GetCoverageScore returns just the coverage score for quick assessment\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetCoverageScore(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"coverage_percent\": 0,\n\t\t\t\"minimum_nodes\":    simulator.MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n\t\t\t\"current_nodes\": suitable for heatmap visualization\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetGDOPHeatmap(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n\t\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"gdop_map\":         []float64{},\n\t\t\t\"grid_dimensions\": []int{0, 0, 0.2,\n\t}\n\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\n\t// Convert results to heatmap format\n\tdepth := len(results)\n\twidth := 0\n\tif depth > 0 {\n\t\twidth = len(results[0])\n\t}\n\n\t// Flatten GDOP values into 1D array (row-major order)\n\tgdopMap := make([]float64, width*depth)\n\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n\t\t\tif math.IsInf(results[y][x].GDOP, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tgdopMap[idx] = 9999.0 // Use 9999 to represent infinity\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tgdopMap[idx] = results[y][x].GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"gdop_map\":         gdopMap,\n\t\t\"grid_dimensions\": []int{width, depth, 1}, // 2D heatmap, so height = 1\n\t\t\"coverage_percent\": gdopComp.CoverageScore(results),\n\t\t\"average_gdop\":     gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results),\n\t\t\"quality_counts\":   gdopComp.QualityCounts(results),\n\t})\n}\n\n// GetShoppingList returns hardware recommendations\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) GetShoppingList(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) SimulationRequest contains parameters for running a simulation\ntype SimulationRequest struct {\n\tDurationSec int     `json:\"duration_sec\"` // Simulation duration in seconds\n\tRateHz      int     `json:\"rate_hz\"`      // Update rate in Hz\n\tThreshold   float64 `json:\"threshold\"`    // DeltaRMS threshold\n}\n\n// SimulationResponse contains simulation results\ntype SimulationResponse `json:\"duration\"`\n\tTicks           int                `json:\"ticks\"`\n}\n\n// RunSimulation runs a time-step simulation with the current configuration\nfunc (h *SimulatorHandler) RunSimulation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req SimulationRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\treq = SimulationRequest{\n\t\t\tDurationSec: 10,\n\t\t\tRateHz:      10,\n\t\t\tThreshold:   0.02,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tspace := h.space\n\tnodes := h.nodes\n\twalkers := h.walkers\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif walkers.Count() == 0 {\n\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"no walkers in simulation\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif nodes.Count() < 2 {\n\t\trespondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"need at least 2 nodes for simulation\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create propagation model\n\tpropModel := simulator.NewPropagationModel(space)\n\n\t// Generate all links\n\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\n\t// Run simulation ticks\n\tdt := 1.0 / float64(req.RateHz)\n\tnumTicks := req.DurationSec * req.RateHz\n\n\t// Collect final positions and link activity\n\tfinalPositions := make([]simulator.Point, 0)\n\tlinkActivity := make(map[string]float64)\n\n\tfor tick := 0; tick < numTicks; tick++ {\n\t\t// Update walker positions\n\t\twalkers.Update(dt, space)\n\n\t\t// Compute link activity\n\t\tfor _, link := range links {\n\t\t\tlinkID := link.TX.ID + \":\" + link.RX.ID\n\t\t\tmaxDelta := 0.0\n\n\t\t\tfor _, walker := range walkers.All() {\n\t\t\t\tdelta := propModel.ComputeLinkActivity(link, walker.Position, req.Threshold)\n\t\t\t\tif delta > maxDelta {\n\t\t\t\t\tmaxDelta = delta\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif maxDelta > linkActivity[linkID] {\n\t\t\t\tlinkActivity[linkID] = maxDelta\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Collect final walker positions\n\tfor _, w := range walkers.All() {\n\t\tfinalPositions = append(finalPositions, w.Position)\n\t}\n\n\tresponse := SimulationResponse{\n\t\tWalkerPositions: finalPositions,\n\t\tLinkActivity:    linkActivity,\n\t\tDuration:        req.DurationSec,\n\t\tTicks:           numTicks,\n\t}\n\n\trespondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// Helper functions\n\nfunc getBoundsJSON(space *simulator.Space) map[string]float64 {\n\tminX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n\treturn http.ResponseWriter, status int, data interface{}) {\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n\tif err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(data); err != nil
